question: "How to operate the boat cross wakes and swells?"
ret: To cross wakes and swells smoothly and safely, follow these steps: 1. Adjust your speed and angle: Change your speed and choose the angle at which you cross the wake or swell. Usually, a slower speed and "quartering" the wake (crossing at an angle) will reduce the jolt. 2. Cross at the least jolt: The best way to cross wakes and swells is with the least jolt to you and the boat. Small swells are not as difficult to cross as larger swells or wakes. Crossing a sharp wake gives more of a jolt than a broad swell. 3. Be prepared to steer and balance: When crossing, you may not notice the disturbance immediately, but at a smaller angle (e.g., 10 degrees), it can be very strong. You must be prepared to steer and balance as necessary. 4. Watch for groups of wakes: Crossing a group of wakes or swells is not as easy or smooth as crossing just one wake. 5. Avoid high-risk maneuvers: Do not jump wakes or waves, as this can increase the risk of back/spinal injury, facial injuries, and broken bones. 6. Stay alert to weather: Be alert to the possibility of adverse weather. Take note of weather forecasts and the prevailing weather conditions before setting out. 7. Ensure safety distance: If you ride further than swimming distance from shore, you should ride by another boat or watercraft, but make sure you stay a safe distance away. 8. Operate with caution: Never operate in water that is less than 60 cm (2 ft) deep, as this increases the chance of hitting a submerged object. Do not operate the watercraft after sunset or before dawn due to the risk of colliding with another boat. <PIC> </PIC> Important Notes: * Always attach the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) to your PFD before operating. * Always wear a USCG-approved PFD when riding in the boat. * Keep both feet on the deck when driving the boat. * Never exceed the maximum load limit (1021 kg / 2250 lb) or allow more than 10 persons (or 9 persons if a wakeboarder/water-skier is being pulled) to ride in the boat at any time. * Keep weight in the boat low and evenly distributed from side-to-side and bow-to-stern. * Remove any unnecessary cargo and store it on shore. * Know and follow U.S. Coast Guard, state, and local laws when operating your boat. * A boating safety course is recommended and may be required in your state.。 To cross wakes and swells smoothly and safely, follow these steps: 1. raw_answer:
结论：
- To cross wakes and swells smoothly and safely, follow these steps:

1.  Adjust your speed and angle: Change your speed and choose the angle at which you cross the wake or swell. Usually, a slower speed and "quartering" the wake (crossing at an angle) will reduce the jolt.
2.  Cross at the least jolt: The best way to cross wakes and swells is with the least jolt to you and the boat. Small swells are not as difficult to cross as larger swells or wakes. Crossing a sharp wake gives more of a jolt than a broad swell.
3.  Be prepared to steer and balance: When crossing, you may not notice the disturbance immediately, but at a smaller angle (e.g., 10 degrees), it can be very strong. You must be prepared to steer and balance as necessary.
4.  Watch for groups of wakes: Crossing a group of wakes or swells is not as easy or smooth as crossing just one wake.
5.  Avoid high-risk maneuvers: Do not jump wakes or waves, as this can increase the risk of back/spinal injury, facial injuries, and broken bones.
6.  Stay alert to weather: Be alert to the possibility of adverse weather. Take note of weather forecasts and the prevailing weather conditions before setting out.
7.  Ensure safety distance: If you ride further than swimming distance from shore, you should ride by another boat or watercraft, but make sure you stay a safe distance away.
8.  Operate with caution: Never operate in water that is less than 60 cm (2 ft) deep, as this increases the chance of hitting a submerged object. Do not operate the watercraft after sunset or before dawn due to the risk of colliding with another boat.

<PIC>
</PIC>

Important Notes:
*   Always attach the engine shut-off cord (lan- yard) to your PFD before operating.
*   Always wear a USCG-approved PFD when riding in the boat.
*   Keep both feet on the deck when driving the boat.
*   Never exceed the maximum load limit (1021 kg / 2250 lb) or allow more than 10 persons (or 9 persons if a wakeboarder/water-skier is being pulled) to ride in the boat at any time.
*   Keep weight in the boat low and evenly distributed from side-to-side and bow-to-stern.
*   Remove any unnecessary cargo and store it on shore.
*   Know and follow U.S. Coast Guard, state, and local laws when operating your boat.
*   A boating safety course is recommended and may be required in your state.。

question: "How do I load the boat when the cruise is over?"
ret: To load the boat after the cruise assist feature is deactivated, follow these steps: 1. Push the plus side or minus side of the no-wake mode/cruise assist switch to deactivate the cruise assist. Once the display changes to "No Wake Mode" or "Cruise", the feature is turned off. 2. Push the remote control levers forward to open the throttle. 3. Adjust the throttle until the desired engine speed is reached. 4. Ensure both engines are operating at the same engine speed before releasing the levers. Important Note: Once the engines have stopped, you have NO STEERING CONTROL over the boat. You could collide with another boat, a dock, or other obstacles. Stopping the engines immediately after operating at high engine speeds is not recommended。 let the engines cool off at idle or low speed for a few minutes first. <PIC> [Image: Boat dashboard showing "No Wake Mode" or "Cruise" indicator] </PIC>。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- To load the boat after the cruise assist feature is deactivated, follow these steps:

1.  Push the plus side or minus side of the no-wake mode/cruise assist switch to deactivate the cruise assist. Once the display changes to "No Wake Mode" or "Cruise", the feature is turned off.
2.  Push the remote control levers forward to open the throttle.
3.  Adjust the throttle until the desired engine speed is reached.
4.  Ensure both engines are operating at the same engine speed before releasing the levers.

Important Note: Once the engines have stopped, you have NO STEERING CONTROL over the boat. You could collide with another boat, a dock, or other obstacles. Stopping the engines immediately after operating at high engine speeds is not recommended。

操作/说明：
- let the engines cool off at idle or low speed for a few minutes first.

question: "How to flush the cooling system of the boat?"
ret: To flush the boat's cooling system, follow these steps: 1. Connect the garden hose to a water tap and attach the garden hose adapter (3) to the hose. 2. Ensure the area around the boat is clear, then start the engine. Immediately after the engine starts, fully turn on the water supply so water flows continuously from the jet thrust nozzle and cooling water pilot outlet. 3. Run the engine at a fast idle for 3 to 5 minutes. If the engine stops during this process, turn off the water supply immediately and repeat the procedure from step 4. 4. Turn off the water supply and drain residual water from the exhaust system by alternately pushing the remote control lever up to half throttle and back to idle for 10 to 15 seconds. 5. Stop the engine. Important Notes: * Never turn on the water before starting the engine, as water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase, causing severe engine damage. * Never have the water on the engine when it is not running, as water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase, causing severe engine damage. * Do not run the engine for more than 15 seconds while water is on. * When checking the engine oil level on water, moor the boat so it will not drift away. * Proper flushing of the cooling system is not possible with the boat in the water. * Extended mooring is not recommended by, but can be performed if proper precautions are taken to help guard against prolonged environmental exposure and marine growth.。 To flush the boat's cooling system, follow these steps: 1. raw_answer:
结论：
- To flush the boat's cooling system, follow these steps:

1. Connect the garden hose to a water tap and attach the garden hose adapter (3) to the hose.
2. Ensure the area around the boat is clear, then start the engine. Immediately after the engine starts, fully turn on the water supply so water flows continuously from the jet thrust nozzle and cooling water pilot outlet.
3. Run the engine at a fast idle for 3 to 5 minutes. If the engine stops during this process, turn off the water supply immediately and repeat the procedure from step 4.
4. Turn off the water supply and drain residual water from the exhaust system by alternately pushing the remote control lever up to half throttle and back to idle for 10 to 15 seconds.
5. Stop the engine.

Important Notes:
*   Never turn on the water before starting the engine, as water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase, causing severe engine damage.
*   Never have the water on the engine when it is not running, as water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase, causing severe engine damage.
*   Do not run the engine for more than 15 seconds while water is on.
*   When checking the engine oil level on water, moor the boat so it will not drift away.
*   Proper flushing of the cooling system is not possible with the boat in the water.
*   Extended mooring is not recommended by, but can be performed if proper precautions are taken to help guard against prolonged environmental exposure and marine growth.。

2. Ensure the area around the boat is clear, then start the engine. Immediately after the engine starts, fully turn on the water supply so water flows continuously from the jet thrust nozzle and cooling water pilot outlet.
3. Run the engine at a fast idle for 3 to 5 minutes. If the engine stops during this process, turn off the water supply immediately and repeat the procedure from step 4.
4. Turn off the water supply and drain residual water from the exhaust system by alternately pushing the remote control lever up to half throttle and back to idle for 10 to 15 seconds.
5. Stop the engine.

Important Notes:
*   Never turn on the water before starting the engine, as water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase, causing severe engine damage.
*   Never have the water on the engine when it is not running, as water could flow back through the muffler into the crankcase, causing severe engine damage.
*   Do not run the engine for more than 15 seconds while water is on.
*   When checking the engine oil level on water, moor the boat so it will not drift away.
*   Proper flushing of the cooling system is not possible with the boat in the water.
*   Extended mooring is not recommended by, but can be performed if proper precautions are taken to help guard against prolonged environmental exposure and marine growth.。

question: "How to check the engine oil level of the boat?"
ret: To check the engine oil level on a boat, follow these steps: 1. Prepare the boat: Ensure the engine is stopped. If checking on land, the engine must be running while water is supplied to the cooling system. If checking on water, moor the boat so it does not drift away. 2. Locate the dipstick: Find the dipstick and the corresponding level marks (Minimum and Maximum) on it. 3. Check the level: * If the engine oil level is below the minimum level mark, slowly add engine oil. * If the engine oil level is significantly above the maximum level mark, consult a Boat dealer. * Continue adding oil until the level is between the minimum and maximum marks. 4. Secure the cap: Securely install the oil tank filler cap and turn it until it stops. 5. Repeat if necessary: Repeat the checking oil level procedure for the other engine. 6. Close the hood: Close the engine hood. Important Notes: * Use only 4-stroke engine oil。 using 2-stroke oil could result in severe engine damage. * Select an oil grade according to the average temperatures in the area where the boat will be used. * If the oil pressure does not rise to specification, a warning will appear on the display and the buzzer will sound. If the oil level is low, add enough oil to raise it to the proper level. If the oil level is sufficient, have a Boat Dealer check the engine.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- To check the engine oil level on a boat, follow these steps:

1.  Prepare the boat: Ensure the engine is stopped. If checking on land, the engine must be running while water is supplied to the cooling system. If checking on water, moor the boat so it does not drift away.
2.  Locate the dipstick: Find the dipstick and the corresponding level marks (Minimum and Maximum) on it.
3.  Check the level:
    *   If the engine oil level is below the minimum level mark, slowly add engine oil.
    *   If the engine oil level is significantly above the maximum level mark, consult a Boat dealer.
    *   Continue adding oil until the level is between the minimum and maximum marks.
4.  Secure the cap: Securely install the oil tank filler cap and turn it until it stops.
5.  Repeat if necessary: Repeat the checking oil level procedure for the other engine.
6.  Close the hood: Close the engine hood.

Important Notes:
*   Use only 4-stroke engine oil。

操作/说明：
- using 2-stroke oil could result in severe engine damage.
*   Select an oil grade according to the average temperatures in the area where the boat will be used.
*   If the oil pressure does not rise to specification, a warning will appear on the display and the buzzer will sound. If the oil level is low, add enough oil to raise it to the proper level. If the oil level is sufficient, have a Boat Dealer check the engine.。

注意事项：
- 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

question: "How to use the energy saving mode of a coffee machine?"
ret: To use the energy saving mode of a coffee machine, follow these steps: 1. Turn the machine off completely. 2. Press and hold the Espresso button for 3 seconds. The Espresso button will blink to indicate the current setting. 3. Press the Espresso button one more time to set the machine to power off after 9 minutes. 4. Press the Espresso button one more time to set the machine to power off after 30 minutes. 5. Press the Lungo button for 3 seconds to exit the energy saving mode. <PIC> [Image of Espresso button blinking and Lungo button] </PIC> Important Notes: Always read all instructions and safety instructions before operating the appliance. Ensure the system is empty before a period of non-use and for frost protection, or before a repair. 3. Place a cup under the coffee outlet.

5. Release the button once the desired volume is served.
6. The water volume level is now stored.。

question: "How should I do if I want to empty the system before not in use, for frost protection or before maintenance?"
ret: To empty the system before a period of non-use or for frost protection, follow these steps: 1. Press both the Espresso and Lungo buttons to turn the machine off. 2. Remove the water tank and open the lever. 3. Press both the Espresso and Lungo buttons for 3 seconds. Both LEDs will blink alternatively. 4. Close the lever. 5. The machine will switch off automatically. 6. Empty and clean the used capsule container and drip tray. <PIC> </PIC> Important Notes: * Block Time: Your machine will be blocked for 10 minutes after emptying mode. * Safety: If you are not familiar with machine servicing, this work should be done by a Boat Dealer or other qualified mechanic. * Maintenance: Do not perform maintenance, replacement, or repair of the emission control devices and system unless otherwise specified. * Warranty: Warranty repair must be performed at an authorized Boat Dealer.。 To empty the system before a period of non-use or for frost protection, follow these steps: 1. question: "How should I do if I want to reset to factory settings of the coffee machine?"
ret: To reset the coffee machine to factory settings, follow these steps: 1. Turn off the machine. 2. Press and hold the Lungo button for 5 seconds. 3. Wait for the LEDs to blink fast 3 times to confirm the reset. 4. The LEDs will then continue to blink normally as the machine heats up, until the steady lights indicate the machine is ready. <PIC> [Reference 1: Image showing the reset procedure with the Lungo button] </PIC> Important Notes: Always read all instructions and safety instructions before operating the appliance. Ensure the machine is turned off before performing the reset. After resetting, the machine will take some time to heat up and reach steady lights before being ready for use.。 To reset the coffee machine to factory settings, follow these steps: 1. question: "How should I clean my coffee maker after I use it so that it can last longer?"
ret: To keep your coffee maker looking new and lasting longer, follow these cleaning steps: 1. Clean the outside: Once a month, clean the outside of the coffee maker with warm soapy water or an abrasive cleaner. 2. Clean the water tank: Rinse the water tank with drinkable water to remove any residue. 3. Clean the lever: Lift the lever completely and insert a capsule. After use, remove the cup and lift the lever to eject the capsule into the used capsule container. 4. Clean the grates: After cooking, scrape the grates with a handled, brass wire bristle brush. 5. Clean the bottom: Check the bottom of the grill for grease buildup and clean it often, especially after cooking fatty meat. 6. Clean the lid and bottom: Apply a strong solution of detergent and water or use a grill cleaner with a scrub brush on the inside of the grill lid and bottom. Rinse and allow to completely air dry. 7. Clean plated wire grates: Wash the grates with concentrated grill cleaner or use a soap and water solution. 8. Clean plastic parts: Wash plastic parts with warm soapy water and wipe dry. Do not use citri sol, abrasive cleaners, degreasers, or a concentrated grill cleaner on plastic parts. 9. Clean porcelain grates: Because of the glaze, clean porcelain grates with concentrated grill cleaner or use a soap and water solution. 10. Dry thoroughly: Dry the grates thoroughly and store them indoors between cookouts. 11. Avoid harsh chemicals: Do not apply a caustic grill/oven cleaner to painted surfaces. 12. Protect the machine: Keep the appliance on a table heat-resistant platform. Do not place it on anything that may block the vents on the bottom of the cooker base or on the air fryer lid. Leave at least 13 cm of space around the air fryer lid when in use. Do not place the appliance on a hot stove. Do not operate the appliance on or near combustible materials such as tablecloths and curtains. 13. Do not cover vents: Do not cover the air vents while the air fryer lid is in operation, as this will prevent even cooking and may cause damage. 14. Do not use unauthorized accessories: Do not use any accessories or attachments not authorized. The use of attachments not recommended by the manufacturer may cause a risk of injury, fire, or electric shock. 15. Prevent hydrostatic lock: When transporting the machine on a trailer or in a truck, ensure the fuel level in the fuel tank is lower than the carburetors. Otherwise, vibration and bumps could cause fuel to flow into the crankcase, resulting in "hydrostatic lock," a condition where the engine cannot rotate. 16. Protect against corrosion: If transporting the machine in an open trailer or truck in areas where road salt is used, coat the metal suspension surface slightly with oil or other protective agents. 17. Clean after transport: Be sure to clean the machine when you get it back. Important Safety Notes: * Do not lift the lever during operation. * Do not cover the air vents while the air fryer lid is in operation. * Do not use abrasive cleaners on plastic parts. * Do not use caustic cleaners on painted surfaces. * Do not operate the appliance on or near combustible materials such as tablecloths and curtains. * Do not place the appliance on a hot stove. * Do not place the appliance on anything that may block the vents on the bottom of the cooker base or on the air fryer lid. * Do not use any accessories or attachments not authorized by the manufacturer.。 To keep your coffee maker looking new and lasting longer, follow these cleaning steps: 1. 5.  Clean the bottom: Check the bottom of the grill for grease buildup and clean it often, especially after cooking fatty meat.
6.  Clean the lid and bottom: Apply a strong solution of detergent and water or use a grill cleaner with a scrub brush on the inside of the grill lid and bottom. Rinse and allow to completely air dry.
7.  Clean plated wire grates: Wash the grates with concentrated grill cleaner or use a soap and water solution.
8.  Clean plastic parts: Wash plastic parts with warm soapy water and wipe dry. Do not use citri sol, abrasive cleaners, degreasers, or a concentrated grill cleaner on plastic parts.
9.  Clean porcelain grates: Because of the glaze, clean porcelain grates with concentrated grill cleaner or use a soap and water solution.
10. Dry thoroughly: Dry the grates thoroughly and store them indoors between cookouts.
11. Avoid harsh chemicals: Do not apply a caustic grill/oven cleaner to painted surfaces.
12. Protect the machine: Keep the appliance on a table heat-resistant platform. Do not place it on anything that may block the vents on the bottom of the cooker base or on the air fryer lid. Leave at least 13 cm of space around the air fryer lid when in use. Do not place the appliance on a hot stove. Do not operate the appliance on or near combustible materials such as tablecloths and curtains.
13. Do not cover vents: Do not cover the air vents while the air fryer lid is in operation, as this will prevent even cooking and may cause damage.
14. Do not use unauthorized accessories: Do not use any accessories or attachments not authorized. The use of attachments not recommended by the manufacturer may cause a risk of injury, fire, or electric shock.
15. Prevent hydrostatic lock: When transporting the machine on a trailer or in a truck, ensure the fuel level in the fuel tank is lower than the carburetors. Otherwise, vibration and bumps could cause fuel to flow into the crankcase, resulting in "hydrostatic lock," a condition where the engine cannot rotate.
16. Protect against corrosion: If transporting the machine in an open trailer or truck in areas where road salt is used, coat the metal suspension surface slightly with oil or other protective agents.
17. Clean after transport: Be sure to clean the machine when you get it back.

Important Safety Notes:
*   Do not lift the lever during operation.
*   Do not cover the air vents while the air fryer lid is in operation.
*   Do not use abrasive cleaners on plastic parts.
*   Do not use caustic cleaners on painted surfaces.
*   Do not operate the appliance on or near combustible materials such as tablecloths and curtains.
*   Do not place the appliance on a hot stove.
*   Do not place the appliance on anything that may block the vents on the bottom of the cooker base or on the air fryer lid.
*   Do not use any accessories or attachments not authorized by the manufacturer.。

question: "What is the maintenance setting screen of the boat controller?"
ret: The maintenance setting screen displays the number of hours that the engines have been running since the last maintenance. After maintenance is performed, you must reset this number of hours of operation to ensure accurate tracking. How to reset the hours of operation: 1. Tap the "Reset" button. 2. When the confirmation message appears, tap the "YES" button to reset the number of hours of operation. Important Safety Note: If you leave the key in the switch, someone could accidentally start the engine and seriously injure you or other bystanders. Therefore, you must remove the key from the switch before performing any maintenance. Additionally, before leaving the operator's position, ensure your hands and feet are away from moving parts or hot surfaces, and do not make adjustments with the engine running.。 The maintenance setting screen displays the number of hours that the engines have been running since the last maintenance. 结论：
- The trip screen displays the hours of engine operation, fuel consumption, and other information. It features a menu with four items displayed at once, which can be scrolled through using the scrollbar.

To reset specific items on the trip screen:
- Touch and hold the item you want to reset for several seconds.
- To reset all items, tap the "Reset" button.

- The trip screen displays the hours of engine operation, fuel consumption, and other information. It features a menu with four items displayed at once, which can be scrolled through using the scrollbar.

To reset specific items on the trip screen:
- Touch and hold the item you want to reset for several seconds.
- To reset all items, tap the "Reset" button.

question: "When I am ready to sail, if everything is ready, how do I make the boat move forward?"
ret: To make the boat move forward when everything is ready, follow these steps: 1. Move the remote control levers to the forward position. 2. Lift the shift gates up slightly from the neutral position to lift the jet thrust nozzles. 3. Ensure the boat is operating at a slow speed. 4. Direct the jet thrust downward at an angle to provide good handling response when turning the steering wheel. When the remote control levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates are lifted all the way up, directing all jet thrust to the rear to move the boat forward. <PIC> [Reference 1] </PIC> Important Notes: * Always anchor from the bow. Anchoring from the stern can make pulling the anchor difficult. * Before starting off, ensure there are no boats, obstructions, or swimmers around you. * Do not attach lifting cables to the bow eye, cleats, stern eyes, ski tow hook, or handgrips. * Remove all people and cargo from the boat before lifting. * When lifting the boat, use a sling-type lifting mechanism designed for boats and attach guidelines to the bow eye and stern eyes to control movement. * Be sure all people are standing clear and the boat is securely held and properly balanced before lifting.。 To make the boat move forward when everything is ready, follow these steps: 1. Move the remote control levers to the forward position. 2. Lift the shift gates up slightly from the neutral position to lift the jet thrust nozzles. 3. Ensure the boat is operating at a slow speed. 4. Direct the jet thrust downward at an angle to provide good handling response when turning the steering wheel. When the remote control levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates are lifted all the way up, directing all jet thrust to the rear to move the boat forward. <PIC> [Reference 1] </PIC> Important Notes: * Always anchor from the bow. Anchoring from the stern can make pulling the anchor difficult. * Before starting off, ensure there are no boats, obstructions, or swimmers around you. * Do not attach lifting cables to the bow eye, cleats, stern eyes, ski tow hook, or handgrips. * Remove all people and cargo from the boat before lifting. * When lifting the boat, use a sling-type lifting mechanism designed for boats and attach guidelines to the bow eye and stern eyes to control movement. * Be sure all people are standing clear and the boat is securely held and properly balanced before lifting.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- To make the boat move forward when everything is ready, follow these steps:

1. Move the remote control levers to the forward position.
2. Lift the shift gates up slightly from the neutral position to lift the jet thrust nozzles.
3. Ensure the boat is operating at a slow speed.
4. Direct the jet thrust downward at an angle to provide good handling response when turning the steering wheel.

When the remote control levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates are lifted all the way up, directing all jet thrust to the rear to move the boat forward.

<PIC>
[Reference 1]
</PIC>

Important Notes:
*   Always anchor from the bow. Anchoring from the stern can make pulling the anchor difficult.
*   Before starting off, ensure there are no boats, obstructions, or swimmers around you.
*   Do not attach lifting cables to the bow eye, cleats, stern eyes, ski tow hook, or handgrips.
*   Remove all people and cargo from the boat before lifting.
*   When lifting the boat, use a sling-type lifting mechanism designed for boats and attach guidelines to the bow eye and stern eyes to control movement.
*   Be sure all people are standing clear and the boat is securely held and properly balanced before lifting.。

question: "How do I check the boat's steering system when I'm driving it?"
ret: To check the boat's steering system while driving, follow these steps: 1. Ensure the steering wheel is secure: Make sure the steering wheel is not loose. There should be no free play in either the in-and-out movement or rotation. 2. Test the steering range: Turn the steering wheel fully to the right and left to ensure operation is smooth and unrestricted throughout the whole range. 3. Verify nozzle alignment: As you turn the steering wheel, ensure that the jet thrust nozzles change direction accordingly. Specifically: When turning right, the jet thrust nozzles should point to starboard (right). When turning left, the jet thrust nozzles should point to port (left). 4. Check for free play: Ensure there is no free play between the steering wheel and the jet thrust nozzles. 5. Monitor keel articulation: Check that the direction of the articulating keel changes according to the movement of the jet thrust nozzles. Important Safety Note: Do not touch the shift gates while the remote control levers are being operated, as you could be pinched. Operate the remote control levers several times to ensure smooth operation throughout the whole range. <PIC> [Image of steering wheel and nozzle alignment during operation] </PIC> Always follow the inspection and maintenance procedures and schedules described in your owner's manual. Pre-operation checks should be made each time the boat is used to ensure safety and reliability.。 To check the boat's steering system while driving, follow these steps: 1. Specifically: When turning right, the jet thrust nozzles should point to starboard (right). When turning left, the jet thrust nozzles should point to port (left). 4. Check for free play: Ensure there is no free play between the steering wheel and the jet thrust nozzles. 5. Monitor keel articulation: Check that the direction of the articulating keel changes according to the movement of the jet thrust nozzles. Pre-operation checks should be made each time the boat is used to ensure safety and reliability.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

结论：
- To check the boat's steering system while driving, follow these steps:

1. Ensure the steering wheel is secure: Make sure the steering wheel is not loose. There should be no free play in either the in-and-out movement or rotation.
2. Test the steering range: Turn the steering wheel fully to the right and left to ensure operation is smooth and unrestricted throughout the whole range.
3. Verify nozzle alignment: As you turn the steering wheel, ensure that the jet thrust nozzles change direction accordingly. Specifically:
   - When turning right, the jet thrust nozzles should point to starboard (right).
   - When turning left, the jet thrust nozzles should point to port (left).
4. Check for free play: Ensure there is no free play between the steering wheel and the jet thrust nozzles.
5. Monitor keel articulation: Check that the direction of the articulating keel changes according to the movement of the jet thrust nozzles.

Important Safety Note: Do not touch the shift gates while the remote control levers are being operated, as you could be pinched. Operate the remote control levers several times to ensure smooth operation throughout the whole range.

<PIC>
[Image of steering wheel and nozzle alignment during operation]
</PIC>

Always follow the inspection and maintenance procedures and schedules described in your owner's manual. Pre-operation checks should be made each time the boat is used to ensure safety and reliability.。

question: "How should fire extinguishers be stored on board and where should they be placed?"
ret: Fire extinguishers should be stored on board in specific compartments depending on the type and quantity: 1. One fire extinguisher: Must be mounted in the lockable storage compartment. Recommended type: Chemical-type extinguisher with a capacity of two pounds or more. 2. Two fire extinguishers (if required): One is mounted in the lockable storage compartment. The second is mounted in the battery compartment. The battery compartment fire extinguisher should be a "clean agent" type (e.g., CO₂ or FE-36™) with a capacity of five pounds or more. Important Notes: Always check that the fire extinguisher is full before use. Ensure the container cap is tightened securely after use. If you do not have a fire extinguisher, contact a local Boat Dealer or fire extinguisher dealer for one meeting proper specifications.。 Fire extinguishers should be stored on board in specific compartments depending on the type and quantity: 1. question: "How to care the throttle-cable of the boat?"
ret: To care for the throttle-cable of the boat, follow these steps: 1. Inspect and Clean: Carefully check the jet intakes under the boat for weeds, debris, or anything else that might restrict the intake of water. If the intakes are clogged, clean them. 2. Grease the Inner Wires: Grease the throttle-cable inner wires at the pulley wheel of the APS. 3. Grease Ball Joints: Grease the steering cable and shift cable ball joints at the jet thrust nozzles. 4. Apply Grease to Inner Wires: Extend the steering cable and shift cable inner wires and apply a thin coat of grease to them. <PIC> </PIC> Important Notes: * Safety Warning: Rotating parts could cause severe injury or death. Before attempting to remove weeds or debris from the jet intakes or impeller areas, shut off the engines, remove the main switch key, and then remove the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) from the engine shut-off switch. * Storage: If the boat will not be used for more than a month, remove the battery and store it in a cool, dark place. * Reference: For detailed information on jet intakes, please refer to page 126.。 To care for the throttle-cable of the boat, follow these steps: 1. Inspect and Clean: Carefully check the jet intakes under the boat for weeds, debris, or anything else that might restrict the intake of water. If the intakes are clogged, clean them. 2. Grease the Inner Wires: Grease the throttle-cable inner wires at the pulley wheel of the APS. 3. Grease Ball Joints: Grease the steering cable and shift cable ball joints at the jet thrust nozzles. 4. Apply Grease to Inner Wires: Extend the steering cable and shift cable inner wires and apply a thin coat of grease to them. <PIC> </PIC> Important Notes: * Safety Warning: Rotating parts could cause severe injury or death. Before attempting to remove weeds or debris from the jet intakes or impeller areas, shut off the engines, remove the main switch key, and then remove the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) from the engine shut-off switch. * Storage: If the boat will not be used for more than a month, remove the battery and store it in a cool, dark place. * Reference: For detailed information on jet intakes, please refer to page 126.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- To care for the throttle-cable of the boat, follow these steps:

1.  Inspect and Clean: Carefully check the jet intakes under the boat for weeds, debris, or anything else that might restrict the intake of water. If the intakes are clogged, clean them.
2.  Grease the Inner Wires: Grease the throttle-cable inner wires at the pulley wheel of the APS.
3.  Grease Ball Joints: Grease the steering cable and shift cable ball joints at the jet thrust nozzles.
4.  Apply Grease to Inner Wires: Extend the steering cable and shift cable inner wires and apply a thin coat of grease to them.

<PIC>
</PIC>

Important Notes:
*   Safety Warning: Rotating parts could cause severe injury or death. Before attempting to remove weeds or debris from the jet intakes or impeller areas, shut off the engines, remove the main switch key, and then remove the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) from the engine shut-off switch.
*   Storage: If the boat will not be used for more than a month, remove the battery and store it in a cool, dark place.
*   Reference: For detailed information on jet intakes, please refer to page 126.。

question: "How do I charge the camera battery when I'm not using it?"
ret: "You can charge the camera battery when it is not in use by using the CB-5L battery charger. Steps to Charge: 1. Connect the Power Cord: Plug the power cord into the CB-5L charger and then into the camera's power port. 2. Attach the Battery: Place the fully charged BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512 battery pack into the charger's battery compartment. 3. Charge: Wait for the charging indicator to show that the battery is fully charged. * BP-511A / BP-514: Approx. 100 minutes. * BP-511 / BP-512: Approx. 90 minutes. 4. Remove the Battery: Once fully charged, remove the battery pack from the charger. Important Notes: * Battery Life: The figures above are based on a fully-charged BP-511A and CIPA (Camera & Imaging Products Association) testing criteria. The actual number of shots may be fewer than indicated above depending on the shooting conditions. * Battery Replacement: If the battery's service life has expired, you must replace it with a new one. * Auto Power Off: If you do not want the camera to turn off by itself, set [f Auto power off] on the menu to [Off]. * Charging Time: The charging time depends on the specific battery model (BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512). * Dimensions: The charger dimensions are approximately 91 x 67 x 32.3 mm (3.6 x 2.6 x 1.3 in). * Compatibility: The CB-5L charger is compatible with the BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512 battery packs. * Safety: When removing the battery from the camera, be sure to reattach the cover to protect against short circuit.。 You can charge the camera battery when it is not in use by using the CB-5L battery charger. raw_answer:
结论：
- You can charge the camera battery when it is not in use by using the CB-5L battery charger.

Steps to Charge:
1.  Connect the Power Cord: Plug the power cord into the CB-5L charger and then into the camera's power port.
2.  Attach the Battery: Place the fully charged BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512 battery pack into the charger's battery compartment.
3.  Charge: Wait for the charging indicator to show that the battery is fully charged.
    *   BP-511A / BP-514: Approx. 100 minutes.
    *   BP-511 / BP-512: Approx. 90 minutes.
4.  Remove the Battery: Once fully charged, remove the battery pack from the charger.

Important Notes:
*   Battery Life: The figures above are based on a fully-charged BP-511A and CIPA (Camera & Imaging Products Association) testing criteria. The actual number of shots may be fewer than indicated above depending on the shooting conditions.
*   Battery Replacement: If the battery's service life has expired, you must replace it with a new one.
*   Auto Power Off: If you do not want the camera to turn off by itself, set [f Auto power off] on the menu to [Off].
*   Charging Time: The charging time depends on the specific battery model (BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512).
*   Dimensions: The charger dimensions are approximately 91 x 67 x 32.3 mm (3.6 x 2.6 x 1.3 in).
*   Compatibility: The CB-5L charger is compatible with the BP-511A, BP-514, BP-511, or BP-512 battery packs.
*   Safety: When removing the battery from the camera, be sure to reattach the cover to protect against short circuit.。

question: "How do I connect the camera using a household power socket?"

Connect the Power Cord: Plug the power cord into the AC adapter kit and then insert the plug into your household power outlet. 3. Turn On the Camera: The camera will operate once the switch is turned on. <PIC> <OFF>: The camera is turned off and does not operate. <ON>: The camera operates. </OFF>:The camera and operate. (p.28) To save battery pwer,the camera turnsoffautomaticaly ater 1 minute of non-operation. raw_answer:
结论：
- You can connect the camera to a household power outlet using the optional AC Adapter Kit ACK-E2. Here are the steps to connect the camera:

1.  Connect the AC Adapter Kit: Attach the AC adapter kit to the camera. This kit includes an AC adapter, a DC coupler, and a power cord.
2.  Connect the Power Cord: Plug the power cord into the AC adapter kit and then insert the plug into your household power outlet.
3.  Turn On the Camera: The camera will operate once the switch is turned on.

<PIC>

<OFF>: The camera is turned off and does not operate.
<ON>: The camera operates.
</OFF>:The camera and operate. (p.28) To save battery pwer,the camera turnsoffautomaticaly ater 1 minute of non-operation. To turn on the camera again, just press the shutter button. ● You can change the auto power-offtime with the menu's [ft Auto power off] setting. (p.33) ·If you turn the switch to while the captured images are being recorded onto the CF card, the remaining number of captured images to be recorded willbe indicated on the top LCD panel with the number of. When all the images are finished recording, the display will turn off and the camera will turn off.。

question: "How do you mount the lens of a camera when preparing for photography?"
ret: "1. Align the Lens Mount: Align the lens with the camera's white EF-S lens mount index and turn the lens as shown by the arrow until it clicks in place. 2. Align for Non-EF-S Lenses: When attaching a lens other than an EF-S lens, align the lens with the red EF lens index mark. 3. Secure the Lens: Press firmly on the lens until it is fully seated. Important Notes: Always attach the lens caps after detaching the lens to avoid scratching the lens surface and electrical contacts. Ensure the lens is aligned correctly before pressing down to ensure a secure fit. For digital cameras with built-in flash, compatible lenses include EF lenses (including EF-S lenses) and CF cards.。 1. raw_answer:
结论：
- 1. Align the Lens Mount: Align the lens with the camera's white EF-S lens mount index and turn the lens as shown by the arrow until it clicks in place.
2. Align for Non-EF-S Lenses: When attaching a lens other than an EF-S lens, align the lens with the red EF lens index mark.
3. Secure the Lens: Press firmly on the lens until it is fully seated.

Important Notes:
- Always attach the lens caps after detaching the lens to avoid scratching the lens surface and electrical contacts.
- Ensure the lens is aligned correctly before pressing down to ensure a secure fit.
- For digital cameras with built-in flash, compatible lenses include EF lenses (including EF-S lenses) and CF cards.。

question: "How do you install the card into a camera before photography?"
ret: To install a card into the camera before photography, follow these steps: 1. Check Compatibility: Ensure the card is a Type I or Type II CF card with a capacity of 2 GB or higher. The camera is also compatible with Microdrive cards of 2 GB or higher capacity. 2. Insert the Card: Insert the card into the camera. <PIC> [Image of CF Card Insertion] </PIC> 3. Secure the Card: Close the camera body to secure the card in place. 4. Verify Installation: Ensure the card is properly seated before proceeding to take photographs. Important Notes: * Do not touch electrical contacts: Do not touch the camera's electrical contacts with your fingers to prevent corrosion, which can cause misoperation. * Avoid physical shock: Do not drop the camera or subject it to physical shock. * Protect from magnetic fields: Never leave the camera near strong magnetic fields (like magnets or electric motors) or strong radio waves (like large antennas), as this can cause misoperation or destroy image data. * Protect from heat: Do not leave the camera in excessive heat, such as in a car in direct sunlight, as high temperatures can cause the camera to malfunction. * Do not disassemble: Never attempt to disassemble the camera yourself. * Use proper cleaning tools: Use a blower to blow away dust on the lens, viewfinder, mirror, and focusing screen. Do not use cleaners that contain organic solvents.。 To install a card into the camera before photography, follow these steps: 1. 3.  Secure the Card: Close the camera body to secure the card in place.
4.  Verify Installation: Ensure the card is properly seated before proceeding to take photographs.

Important Notes:
*   Do not touch electrical contacts: Do not touch the camera's electrical contacts with your fingers to prevent corrosion, which can cause misoperation.
*   Avoid physical shock: Do not drop the camera or subject it to physical shock.
*   Protect from magnetic fields: Never leave the camera near strong magnetic fields (like magnets or electric motors) or strong radio waves (like large antennas), as this can cause misoperation or destroy image data.
*   Protect from heat: Do not leave the camera in excessive heat, such as in a car in direct sunlight, as high temperatures can cause the camera to malfunction.
*   Do not disassemble: Never attempt to disassemble the camera yourself.
*   Use proper cleaning tools: Use a blower to blow away dust on the lens, viewfinder, mirror, and focusing screen. Do not use cleaners that contain organic solvents.。

question: "How do I replace the camera's date/time battery?"
ret: "To replace the camera's date/time battery, follow these steps: 1. Remove the battery cover: Open the cover to access the battery compartment. 2. Remove the old battery: Take out the existing CR2016 lithium battery. 3. Insert the new battery: Load a fully charged CR2016 lithium battery into the camera. 4. Reattach the cover: Securely reattach the cover to protect against short circuits. 5. Set the date and time: Since the replacement resets the date and time, you must manually set the correct date and time on the camera. Important Notes: * Battery Type: Use a new CR2016 lithium battery. * Power Off: The camera will turn off and the shutter will close immediately after the battery is removed. Do not open the battery compartment or CF card slot during cleaning if the power is off to prevent damage. * Cleaning: Do not insert the blower tip inside the camera beyond the lens mount, and never use canned air or a brush on the sensor. * Battery Life: The battery typically lasts about 5 years.。 To replace the camera's date/time battery, follow these steps: 1. Remove the battery cover: Open the cover to access the battery compartment. 2. Remove the old battery: Take out the existing CR2016 lithium battery. 3. Insert the new battery: Load a fully charged CR2016 lithium battery into the camera. 4. Reattach the cover: Securely reattach the cover to protect against short circuits. 5. Set the date and time: Since the replacement resets the date and time, you must manually set the correct date and time on the camera. Important Notes: * Battery Type: Use a new CR2016 lithium battery. * Power Off: The camera will turn off and the shutter will close immediately after the battery is removed. Do not open the battery compartment or CF card slot during cleaning if the power is off to prevent damage. * Cleaning: Do not insert the blower tip inside the camera beyond the lens mount, and never use canned air or a brush on the sensor. * Battery Life: The battery typically lasts about 5 years.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准<PIC><PIC>。";["Manual23_15", "Manual23_26"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- To replace the camera's date/time battery, follow these steps:

1.  Remove the battery cover: Open the cover to access the battery compartment.
2.  Remove the old battery: Take out the existing CR2016 lithium battery.
3.  Insert the new battery: Load a fully charged CR2016 lithium battery into the camera.
4.  Reattach the cover: Securely reattach the cover to protect against short circuits.
5.  Set the date and time: Since the replacement resets the date and time, you must manually set the correct date and time on the camera.

Important Notes:
*   Battery Type: Use a new CR2016 lithium battery.
*   Power Off: The camera will turn off and the shutter will close immediately after the battery is removed. Do not open the battery compartment or CF card slot during cleaning if the power is off to prevent damage.
*   Cleaning: Do not insert the blower tip inside the camera beyond the lens mount, and never use canned air or a brush on the sensor.
*   Battery Life: The battery typically lasts about 5 years.。

question: "How to set an off-center subject mode of a camera?"
ret: "To set an off-center subject mode on this camera, follow these steps: 1. Select the desired shooting mode from the Mode Dial. This includes Basic Zone Modes, Creative Zone Modes (such as P, Tv, Av, M, A-DEP), or specific recording quality modes (L/L/M/M/S/S (JPEG)). 2. Adjust the AF mode to suit the subject. In Basic Zone modes, the AF mode is set automatically, but in Creative Zone modes, you can choose between One-Shot AF (for still subjects) or AI Servo AF (for moving subjects). 3. Select the AF point by turning the Mode Dial until the desired point lights up. This allows you to shoot with autofocus while framing the subject as desired. 4. Choose the metering mode that suits the subject or your photographic intention. In Basic Zone modes, the metering mode is set automatically, but in Creative Zone modes, you can select between Evaluative, partial, and centerweighted average metering modes. 5. Select the drive mode that suits the subject or your photographic intention. In Basic Zone modes, the drive mode is set automatically, but in Creative Zone modes, you can choose between Single, continuous, and Self-timer drive modes. Note: In Basic Zone modes, all settings are set automatically to suit the subject, allowing you to simply point and shoot. In Creative Zone modes, you have more control over the settings. <PIC></PIC> <PIC></PIC> Always ensure the camera is in a stable position and avoid physical shock to prevent malfunction. 2. Adjust the AF mode to suit the subject. In Basic Zone modes, the AF mode is set automatically, but in Creative Zone modes, you can choose between One-Shot AF (for still subjects) or AI Servo AF (for moving subjects).

question: "Before taking photos, we need to know How to use the eyepiece cover of the camera?"
ret: "To use the eyepiece cover during self-timer or remote switch (optional) operation, please follow these steps: 1. Check if your eye covers the viewfinder eyepiece: If your eye does not cover the viewfinder eyepiece, stray light may enter the eyepiece and affect the exposure when the image is captured. 2. Attach the eyepiece cover: Use the eyepiece cover (refer to page 17) to prevent stray light. 3. Attach the strap: Pass the end of the strap through the camera's strap mount from the bottom. Then pass it through the strap's buckle as shown in the illustration. Pull the strap to take up any slack and make sure the strap will not loosen from the buckle. The eyepiece cover is also attached to the strap. <PIC> </PIC> Important Notes: * Do not use the camera if condensation forms on the camera. If this occurs, remove the lens, CF card, and battery from the camera, and wait until the condensation has evaporated before using the camera. * Do not connect or disconnect the power cord while the camera's switch is set to a specific position (refer to page 89 for the strap attachment). * Protect against short circuits: When removing the battery, be sure to reattach the cover to protect against short circuit (refer to page 89).。 To use the eyepiece cover during self-timer or remote switch (optional) operation, please follow these steps: 1. raw_answer:
结论：
- To use the eyepiece cover during self-timer or remote switch (optional) operation, please follow these steps:

1.  Check if your eye covers the viewfinder eyepiece: If your eye does not cover the viewfinder eyepiece, stray light may enter the eyepiece and affect the exposure when the image is captured.
2.  Attach the eyepiece cover: Use the eyepiece cover (refer to page 17) to prevent stray light.
3.  Attach the strap: Pass the end of the strap through the camera's strap mount from the bottom. Then pass it through the strap's buckle as shown in the illustration. Pull the strap to take up any slack and make sure the strap will not loosen from the buckle. The eyepiece cover is also attached to the strap.

Important Notes:
*   Do not use the camera if condensation forms on the camera. If this occurs, remove the lens, CF card, and battery from the camera, and wait until the condensation has evaporated before using the camera.
*   Do not connect or disconnect the power cord while the camera's switch is set to a specific position (refer to page 89 for the strap attachment).
*   Protect against short circuits: When removing the battery, be sure to reattach the cover to protect against short circuit (refer to page 89).。

2.  Attach the eyepiece cover: Use the eyepiece cover (refer to page 17) to prevent stray light.
3.  Attach the strap: Pass the end of the strap through the camera's strap mount from the bottom. Then pass it through the strap's buckle as shown in the illustration. Pull the strap to take up any slack and make sure the strap will not loosen from the buckle. The eyepiece cover is also attached to the strap.

question: "How do I select the AF Mode when before taking a picture?"
ret: "To select the AF (Autofocus) mode before taking a picture, follow these steps: 1. Turn the Mode Dial: Look at the LCD panel and turn the dial (labeled with <>) to select the desired AF mode. The available modes are: * ONE-Shot AF: Suitable for still subjects. * AI Focus AF: Automatically switches from One-Shot AF to AI Servo AF if the subject starts moving. * AI Servo AF: Best for moving subjects. *(Refer to and for details on these modes.)* 2. Select the AF Point: While looking at the LCD panel, turn the dial again to select the specific AF point where you want to focus. The viewfinder typically shows 9 AF points. *(Refer to for instructions on moving the AF point over the subject.)* 3. Confirm the Setting: Once the AF mode and point are selected, the camera is ready to focus. *(Refer to for details on how the camera determines the optimum AF mode in Basic Zone modes.)* Important Note: If an Extender is attached and the maximum aperture of the lens is smaller than the Extender's aperture, AF may not be possible. Please refer to the Extender's instructions for more details. *(Refer to for details on Extenders.)*。 To select the AF (Autofocus) mode before taking a picture, follow these steps: 1. The available modes are: * ONE-Shot AF: Suitable for still subjects. * AI Focus AF: Automatically switches from One-Shot AF to AI Servo AF if the subject starts moving. * AI Servo AF: Best for moving subjects. *(Refer to and for details on these modes.)* 2. Select the AF Point: While looking at the LCD panel, turn the dial again to select the specific AF point where you want to focus. The viewfinder typically shows 9 AF points. *(Refer to for instructions on moving the AF point over the subject.)* 3. Confirm the Setting: Once the AF mode and point are selected, the camera is ready to focus. *(Refer to for details on how the camera determines the optimum AF mode in Basic Zone modes.)* Important Note: If an Extender is attached and the maximum aperture of the lens is smaller than the Extender's aperture, AF may not be possible. Please refer to the Extender's instructions for more details. *(Refer to for details on Extenders.)*。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准<PIC><PIC><PIC><PIC>。";["Manual23_124", "Manual23_131", "Manual23_120", "Manual23_121"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- To select the AF (Autofocus) mode before taking a picture, follow these steps:

1.  Turn the Mode Dial: Look at the LCD panel and turn the dial (labeled with <>) to select the desired AF mode. The available modes are:
    *   ONE-Shot AF: Suitable for still subjects.
    *   AI Focus AF: Automatically switches from One-Shot AF to AI Servo AF if the subject starts moving.
    *   AI Servo AF: Best for moving subjects.
    *(Refer to [参考2] and [参考5] for details on these modes.)*

2.  Select the AF Point: While looking at the LCD panel, turn the dial again to select the specific AF point where you want to focus. The viewfinder typically shows 9 AF points.
    *(Refer to [参考4] for instructions on moving the AF point over the subject.)*

3.  Confirm the Setting: Once the AF mode and point are selected, the camera is ready to focus.
    *(Refer to [参考5] for details on how the camera determines the optimum AF mode in Basic Zone modes.)*

Important Note: If an Extender is attached and the maximum aperture of the lens is smaller than the Extender's aperture, AF may not be possible. Please refer to the Extender's instructions for more details.
*(Refer to [参考2] for details on Extenders.)*。

    *   AI Focus AF: Automatically switches from One-Shot AF to AI Servo AF if the subject starts moving.
    *   AI Servo AF: Best for moving subjects.
    *(Refer to [参考2] and [参考5] for details on these modes.)*

2.  Select the AF Point: While looking at the LCD panel, turn the dial again to select the specific AF point where you want to focus. The viewfinder typically shows 9 AF points.
    *(Refer to [参考4] for instructions on moving the AF point over the subject.)*

3.  Confirm the Setting: Once the AF mode and point are selected, the camera is ready to focus.
    *(Refer to [参考5] for details on how the camera determines the optimum AF mode in Basic Zone modes.)*

Important Note: If an Extender is attached and the maximum aperture of the lens is smaller than the Extender's aperture, AF may not be possible. Please refer to the Extender's instructions for more details.
*(Refer to [参考2] for details on Extenders.)*。

question: "How can I erase all images from my camera if I don't like these."
ret: "To erase all images from your camera, follow these steps: 1. Eject the CF card: Press the Eject button to remove the CF card. 2. Close the cover: Close the cover of the CF card slot. 3. Turn off the camera: Press the Power Switch to turn the camera off. 4. Erase the data: While the camera is off, press and hold the Eject button for 10 seconds. 5. Eject the card: Press the Eject button again to eject the CF card. Note: If you use a Microdrive, be careful not to subject the camera to vibration or physical shock while recording or displaying images. <PIC></PIC>。 To erase all images from your camera, follow these steps: 1. question: "How do I print photos using the camera's CP direct method after taking them?"
ret: "To print photos using the camera's CP (Camera Print) direct method, follow these steps: 1. Connect the Camera to the Printer: Connect the camera directly to a printer capable of direct printing via the Bubble Jet Direct standard. 2. Set Communication Mode: On the camera's menu, set the [ft Communication] setting to [PTP]. 3. Insert a CF Card: Insert a CF card into the camera. 4. Take the Photos: Press the shutter button completely to take the picture. 5. Print the Photos: Once the photos are taken, you can print them directly from the camera's LCD monitor using the connected printer. <PIC> </PIC> Important Notes: * Image Format: If the image is captured with the colorspace set to Adobe RGB, the file name will start with `u\_M\_sfG\_ ^ \ \ \ \\mathfrak n`. Post-processing with software may be required for images captured in this mode to ensure they look good on standard computers and printers. * DPOF Support: If your camera supports DPOF (Digital Print Order Format), you can specify which photos and quantity to print by inserting a CF card into a DPOF-compatible printer. * Camera Shake: To prevent camera shake, hold the camera steady with your right hand, place your finger tip on the shutter button, and press it gently. Pressing halfway and then completely immediately can cause camera shake.。 To print photos using the camera's CP (Camera Print) direct method, follow these steps: 1. raw_answer:
结论：
- To print photos using the camera's CP (Camera Print) direct method, follow these steps:

1.  Connect the Camera to the Printer: Connect the camera directly to a printer capable of direct printing via the Bubble Jet Direct standard.
2.  Set Communication Mode: On the camera's menu, set the [ft Communication] setting to [PTP].
3.  Insert a CF Card: Insert a CF card into the camera.
4.  Take the Photos: Press the shutter button completely to take the picture.
5.  Print the Photos: Once the photos are taken, you can print them directly from the camera's LCD monitor using the connected printer.

<PIC>
</PIC>

Important Notes:
*   Image Format: If the image is captured with the colorspace set to Adobe RGB, the file name will start with `u\_M\_sfG\_ - ^ \ \ \ \\mathfrak n`. Post-processing with software may be required for images captured in this mode to ensure they look good on standard computers and printers.
*   DPOF Support: If your camera supports DPOF (Digital Print Order Format), you can specify which photos and quantity to print by inserting a CF card into a DPOF-compatible printer.
*   Camera Shake: To prevent camera shake, hold the camera steady with your right hand, place your finger tip on the shutter button, and press it gently. Pressing halfway and then completely immediately can cause camera shake.。

question: "If the case battery of earphones is low, how can I charge is?"
ret: "To charge the case battery when it is low, follow these steps: 1. Insert the USB-C cable into the charging port located at the back of the charging case. 2. Insert the standard USB cable into any suitable USB port on your device. 3. The Battery Level LED Indicators will light up according to the battery capacity. 4. You can see the case battery level at any time by pressing the button on the right hand side of the case. <PIC> Please note that the charging case holds 3 charges, giving a total playtime of approximately 36 hours. For the fastest charging, use the Fast Charge Time feature which takes 10 minutes for 2 hours of charging.。 To charge the case battery when it is low, follow these steps: 1. The Battery Level LED Indicators will light up according to the battery capacity. 4. You can see the case battery level at any time by pressing the button on the right hand side of the case. <PIC> Please note that the charging case holds 3 charges, giving a total playtime of approximately 36 hours. For the fastest charging, use the Fast Charge Time feature which takes 10 minutes for 2 hours of charging.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。";["Manual23_26", "Manual23_7"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- To charge the case battery when it is low, follow these steps:

1. Insert the USB-C cable into the charging port located at the back of the charging case.
2. Insert the standard USB cable into any suitable USB port on your device.
3. The Battery Level LED Indicators will light up according to the battery capacity.
4. You can see the case battery level at any time by pressing the button on the right hand side of the case.

<PIC>

Please note that the charging case holds 3 charges, giving a total playtime of approximately 36 hours. For the fastest charging, use the Fast Charge Time feature which takes 10 minutes for 2 hours of charging.。

question: "When bluetooth are pairing and connecting, what scenarios I might encounter?"
ret: When Bluetooth is pairing and connecting, you might encounter the following scenarios: 1. Automatic Reconnection: If your earbuds have been previously paired to a nearby device, they will automatically re-connect to it without requiring a new pairing process. 2. Connection Failure: If the connection fails, ensure that Bluetooth is turned ON in your phone's settings before attempting to connect to the available device. 3. Pairing Issues: If the connection is not successful, check for any interference or obstructions that might prevent a stable Bluetooth signal from being established. question: "Besides the two main controling functions, what other functions of the earphones can I operate?"
ret: "Based on the provided references, the earphones have the following additional functions beyond the two main controling functions (Power Switch and Dial): 1. White Balance Auto Bracketing: This feature allows the camera to capture multiple images with different white balance settings in a single burst. * Operation: Set "BKT" to $\pm 0^\circ$ (specifically $\pm 0^\circ$ or $\pm 0.5^\circ$ depending on the manual version, as indicated by the reference text). * Limitations: White balance bracketing will not work if the image-recording quality is set to RAW or RAW+JPEG. * Burst Limits: The maximum burst is 6 shots. If combined with white balance correction and AEB shooting, a total of 9 images will be recorded for a single shot. * Visual Indicator: When set, the white balance icon will blink on the LCD panel, and the remaining shots will decrease to about 1/3. 2. Auto Power-off Time: The camera automatically turns off after 1 minute of non-operation. * Customization: You can change this setting via the menu's [ft Auto power off] setting. * Reactivation: To turn the camera back on, simply press the shutter button. 3. Image Recording Status Display: The top LCD panel displays the remaining number of captured images to be recorded when the switch is turned on. * Visual Indicator: When all images are finished recording, the display turns off and the camera turns off. 4. Main Dial and Quick Control Dial: While the references mention these icons, they describe their function as setting exposure compensation or aperture in the mode, which are part of the main control functions (Power Switch and Dial). 5. Menu and Custom Functions: The Quick Control Dial is used for menu functions and Custom Functions. 6. Power On/Off: * Power On: Turn the switch to ON. * Power Off: Turn the switch to OFF. * Note: The camera operates only after the switch is turned on. 7. Interference Correction: If the equipment causes harmful interference, the user can try to correct it by reorienting or relocating the receiving antenna, increasing the separation between the equipment and receiver, or connecting the equipment differently (though the specific connection method is cut off in the provided text). 8. CF Card Recording: The camera records images onto a CF card. - Based on the provided references, the earphones have the following additional functions beyond the two main controling functions (Power Switch and Dial):

1.  White Balance Auto Bracketing: This feature allows the camera to capture multiple images with different white balance settings in a single burst.
    *   Operation: Set "BKT" to $\pm 0^\circ$ (specifically $\pm 0^\circ$ or $\pm 0.5^\circ$ depending on the manual version, as indicated by the reference text).
    *   Limitations: White balance bracketing will not work if the image-recording quality is set to RAW or RAW+JPEG.
    *   Burst Limits: The maximum burst is 6 shots. If combined with white balance correction and AEB shooting, a total of 9 images will be recorded for a single shot.
    *   Visual Indicator: When set, the white balance icon will blink on the LCD panel, and the remaining shots will decrease to about 1/3.

2.  Auto Power-off Time: The camera automatically turns off after 1 minute of non-operation.
    *   Customization: You can change this setting via the menu's [ft Auto power off] setting.
    *   Reactivation: To turn the camera back on, simply press the shutter button.

3.  Image Recording Status Display: The top LCD panel displays the remaining number of captured images to be recorded when the switch is turned on.
    *   Visual Indicator: When all images are finished recording, the display turns off and the camera turns off.

4.  Main Dial and Quick Control Dial: While the references mention these icons, they describe their function as setting exposure compensation or aperture in the mode, which are part of the main control functions (Power Switch and Dial).

5.  Menu and Custom Functions: The Quick Control Dial is used for menu functions and Custom Functions.

6.  Power On/Off:
    *   Power On: Turn the switch to ON.
    *   Power Off: Turn the switch to OFF.
    *   Note: The camera operates only after the switch is turned on.

7.  Interference Correction: If the equipment causes harmful interference, the user can try to correct it by reorienting or relocating the receiving antenna, increasing the separation between the equipment and receiver, or connecting the equipment differently (though the specific connection method is cut off in the provided text).

8.  CF Card Recording: The camera records images onto a CF card. If the switch is turned on while images are being recorded, the remaining number of images will be indicated on the top LCD panel.。

question: "If my earphones won't work, how can I reset them?"
ret: "To reset your earphones, please follow these steps: 1. Disconnect and Remove: Disconnect the earphones from Bluetooth and remove them from their case. 2. Perform Factory Reset: Press and hold the reset button for 6 seconds. This will erase all settings. 3. Perform Hardware Reset: If the earphones are unresponsive, press and hold the reset button for 10 seconds. Press again to power on. * *Note*: Hardware resets are only necessary in the rare case that the earbuds lock up and are unresponsive. 4. Reconnect: After the reset, turn Bluetooth ON on your phone/device and pair the earphones again. <PIC></PIC>。 To reset your earphones, please follow these steps: 1. Disconnect and Remove: Disconnect the earphones from Bluetooth and remove them from their case. 2. Perform Factory Reset: Press and hold the reset button for 6 seconds. This will erase all settings. 3. Perform Hardware Reset: If the earphones are unresponsive, press and hold the reset button for 10 seconds. Press again to power on. * *Note*: Hardware resets are only necessary in the rare case that the earbuds lock up and are unresponsive. 4. Reconnect: After the reset, turn Bluetooth ON on your phone/device and pair the earphones again. <PIC></PIC>。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。";["Manual23_117"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- To reset your earphones, please follow these steps:

1.  Disconnect and Remove: Disconnect the earphones from Bluetooth and remove them from their case.
2.  Perform Factory Reset: Press and hold the reset button for 6 seconds. This will erase all settings.
3.  Perform Hardware Reset: If the earphones are unresponsive, press and hold the reset button for 10 seconds. Press again to power on.
    *   *Note*: Hardware resets are only necessary in the rare case that the earbuds lock up and are unresponsive.
4.  Reconnect: After the reset, turn Bluetooth ON on your phone/device and pair the earphones again.

question: "What are the maintenance and care policies of the earphones?"
ret: Cleaning: Use a soft cloth or paper towel to clean your earphones. Never use any harsh chemicals or detergents for cleaning. Make sure your earphones are dry before charging. Storage: When your earphones are not in use, they should be stored in a cool, dry place. Cable Care: Never tug or yank on a cable while it is connected to your charging case. Connect and disconnect cables as carefully as possible. Environment: Never expose your earphones to high temperatures or extreme cold. Recycling: Please recycle or dispose of your earphones properly based on the laws and rules of your municipality. Contact local recycling facilities and/or the manufacturer of your earphones for further information.。 Cleaning: Use a soft cloth or paper towel to clean your earphones. Never use any harsh chemicals or detergents for cleaning. Make sure your earphones are dry before charging. Storage: When your earphones are not in use, they should be stored in a cool, dry place. Contact local recycling facilities and/or the manufacturer of your earphones for further information.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

Cable Care: Never tug or yank on a cable while it is connected to your charging case. Connect and disconnect cables as carefully as possible.

Environment: Never expose your earphones to high temperatures or extreme cold.

Recycling: Please recycle or dispose of your earphones properly based on the laws and rules of your municipality. Contact local recycling facilities and/or the manufacturer of your earphones for further information.。

question: "From three different observing views, what buttons and interfaces should this eReader have?"
ret: Based on the provided reference materials, the eReader has the following buttons and interfaces for observing content from different views: Buttons: * Rotate: Used to rotate the screen horizontally or vertically for convenience when in e-Book mode. * Brightness: Used to select the desired comfortable brightness level (5 levels available). * Set Color: Used to select the comfortable color word to read. * Previous/Next Page: Used to turn pages. * Navigation: Up and down arrows (implied by "Navigation control") to do the same task as Previous/Next Page. Interfaces: * e-Book Mode: Allows the user to rotate the screen horizontally or vertically. * Browser Mode: Features two browser options: "Auto Mode" and "Manual Mode". * Bookmark List: A list of saved bookmarks pops out, allowing the user to select and delete them. * LCD Panel: Located on the top panel to check the remaining number of images the CF card can record. <PIC> <ref>Reference 3: Page Jump, Save Mark, Load Mark, Del Mark, Browser Mode</ref> </PIC> <ref>Reference 4: Connection of external equipment (some models)</ref> <ref>Reference 5: e Book Compatible Format</ref> <ref>Reference 1: MENU White Balance Auto Bracketing</ref>。 Based on the provided reference materials, the eReader has the following buttons and interfaces for observing content from different views: Buttons: * Rotate: Used to rotate the screen horizontally or vertically for convenience when in e-Book mode. * Brightness: Used to select the desired comfortable brightness level (5 levels available). * Set Color: Used to select the comfortable color word to read. * Previous/Next Page: Used to turn pages. * Navigation: Up and down arrows (implied by "Navigation control") to do the same task as Previous/Next Page. Interfaces: * e-Book Mode: Allows the user to rotate the screen horizontally or vertically. * Browser Mode: Features two browser options: "Auto Mode" and "Manual Mode". * Bookmark List: A list of saved bookmarks pops out, allowing the user to select and delete them. * LCD Panel: Located on the top panel to check the remaining number of images the CF card can record. <PIC> <ref>Reference 3: Page Jump, Save Mark, Load Mark, Del Mark, Browser Mode</ref> </PIC> <ref>Reference 4: Connection of external equipment (some models)</ref> <ref>Reference 5: e Book Compatible Format</ref> <ref>Reference 1: MENU White Balance Auto Bracketing</ref>。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

question: "What do the functions of Main Menu and Browser History of the eReader can do?"
ret: Main Menu and Browser History of the eReader can do the following: 1. Main Menu: Displays all features of the device, including: Browser Book History eBook Music Video Photo Record Explorer Settings Users can select a desired function and enter to access it. 2. Browser History: Displays the file recently browsed. To select a book, move the Navigation keys to the "M" button. Pressing the "M" button enters the last page you read on the selected book. Note: If files are on the Micro SD card, recently browsed files are deleted, but records remain in the "Browser history" list and cannot be opened. 3. Record Playback Function: Go to main menu and select "Music": Press the "M" button to go to the Music Menu. Select "Record": Press the "M" button to go to the "Recorded" file list. Select a desired recorded file: Press the "M" button to start playback.。 Main Menu and Browser History of the eReader can do the following: 1. Main Menu: Displays all features of the device, including: Browser Book History eBook Music Video Photo Record Explorer Settings Users can select a desired function and enter to access it. 2. Browser History: Displays the file recently browsed. To select a book, move the Navigation keys to the "M" button. Pressing the "M" button enters the last page you read on the selected book. Note: If files are on the Micro SD card, recently browsed files are deleted, but records remain in the "Browser history" list and cannot be opened. 3. Record Playback Function: Go to main menu and select "Music": Press the "M" button to go to the Music Menu. Select "Record": Press the "M" button to go to the "Recorded" file list. Select a desired recorded file: Press the "M" button to start playback.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- Main Menu and Browser History of the eReader can do the following:

1. Main Menu:
   - Displays all features of the device, including:
     - Browser
     - Book History
     - eBook
     - Music
     - Video
     - Photo
     - Record
     - Explorer
     - Settings
   - Users can select a desired function and enter to access it.

2. Browser History:
   - Displays the file recently browsed.
   - To select a book, move the Navigation keys to the "M" button.
   - Pressing the "M" button enters the last page you read on the selected book.
   - Note: If files are on the Micro SD card, recently browsed files are deleted, but records remain in the "Browser history" list and cannot be opened.

3. Record Playback Function:
   - Go to main menu and select "Music": Press the "M" button to go to the Music Menu.
   - Select "Record": Press the "M" button to go to the "Recorded" file list.
   - Select a desired recorded file: Press the "M" button to start playback.。

question: "If I want to listen to music using this eReader, what should I do?"
ret: To listen to music using this eReader, follow these steps: 1. Open the Music menu from the main menu. 2. Select the desired audio file from the list. 3. Press the "M" key to enter Music mode. 4. Adjust the volume using the Up/Down keys or the Up/Prev page and Down/Next page keys. 5. Press the "Play / Pause" key to start or stop playback. 6. Use the left direction key to go backward and the right direction key to go forward through the music files. 7. Press and hold the left direction key to go to the previous file, or press and release the right direction key to go to the next file. Note: You can also connect the device to your computer via a USB cable and drag audio files into the device to add them to the list. The device supports MP3, WMA, WAC, ACC, and ECT formats.。 To listen to music using this eReader, follow these steps: 1. Open the Music menu from the main menu. 2. Press the "Play / Pause" key to start or stop playback. 6. Use the left direction key to go backward and the right direction key to go forward through the music files. 7. Press and hold the left direction key to go to the previous file, or press and release the right direction key to go to the next file. Note: You can also connect the device to your computer via a USB cable and drag audio files into the device to add them to the list. The device supports MP3, WMA, WAC, ACC, and ECT formats.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
