3.  Press the "M" key to enter Music mode.
4.  Adjust the volume using the Up/Down keys or the Up/Prev page and Down/Next page keys.
5.  Press the "Play / Pause" key to start or stop playback.
6.  Use the left direction key to go backward and the right direction key to go forward through the music files.
7.  Press and hold the left direction key to go to the previous file, or press and release the right direction key to go to the next file.

Note: You can also connect the device to your computer via a USB cable and drag audio files into the device to add them to the list. The device supports MP3, WMA, WAC, ACC, and ECT formats.。

question: "Can this eReader become a photo viewer? If so, how do I operate this feature?"
ret: "Can this eReader become a photo viewer? Yes, this eReader can become a photo viewer. How to use it: 1. Enter Photo Mode: Press the "M" button on the device to enter Photo mode. 2. View Photos: Once in Photo mode, you can view JPEG and BMP image files. 3. Navigate: Use the "left / right" navigation keys to go to the Previous or Next photo. 4. Rotate: Press the "Play/Pause" key to rotate the photo counter-clockwise. 5. Zoom: Press the zoom keys to zoom in or out of the photo. Note: Some large size files may require more time to rotate and load into the photo viewer compared to small files. <PIC>。 Yes, this eReader can become a photo viewer. How to use it: 1. question: "When using this eReader, what kinds of trobles might I encounter and how to solve them?"
ret: "When using this E Reader, you might encounter the following issues and their solutions: Troubleshooting Micro SD Card Issues * Problem: Device cannot identify the inserted Micro SD card. * Solution: Turn off the device, pull the memory card out, and reinsert it. Ensure the device is powered off during this process. Do not reset the unit while the card is inserted. * Note: Always keep the device covered when not in use to protect the screen. Troubleshooting Power and Reset * Problem: Device is not turning on or needs a reset. * Solution: Press the Power button for 3-5 seconds to turn it on/off. If the device is unresponsive or has minor problems, use the Reset On/Off switch located on the back of the device next to the card reader to reset it back to the factory default. Troubleshooting Menu and Navigation * Problem: Cannot exit the menu or navigate the screen. * Solution: Press the <MENU> button to exit the menu display. If the device is in Basic Zone mode, certain menu items (like RAW and RAW JPEG recording quality) will not be displayed. You can also use the < > dial to select menu items or playback images. * Note: Menu operation works while the image is being recorded to the CF card (access lamp blinks). Troubleshooting Audio and Display * Problem: No sound or display issues. * Solution: Check the volume levels using the ^+ (UP) and ^ (DOWN) buttons. If the display is damaged or difficult to read, cover the device to protect the screen. * Note: The device features a 7" TFT LCD Display Screen. Troubleshooting Physical Damage and Care * Problem: Device is damaged or damaged by external factors. * Solution: Avoid putting unnecessary pressure on the device or striking it against other objects. Do not drop, crush, bend, or apply excessive force. * Prevention: Keep the device covered when not in use. Do not expose it to moisture, extreme temperatures, or harmful conditions. Clean the device with a soft, dry cloth。 do not use harsh chemicals. Troubleshooting Charging and Connectivity * Problem: Device is not charging or data transfer fails. * Solution: Use the USB Port with a USB cable to charge the device or connect to a computer to transfer contents. * Note: The device supports Micro SD/SDHC cards up to 16GB.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准<PIC><PIC><PIC>。";["Manual23_48", "Manual23_49", "Manual23_52"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- When using this E Reader, you might encounter the following issues and their solutions:

Troubleshooting Micro SD Card Issues
*   Problem: Device cannot identify the inserted Micro SD card.
*   Solution: Turn off the device, pull the memory card out, and reinsert it. Ensure the device is powered off during this process. Do not reset the unit while the card is inserted.
*   Note: Always keep the device covered when not in use to protect the screen.

Troubleshooting Power and Reset
*   Problem: Device is not turning on or needs a reset.
*   Solution: Press the Power button for 3-5 seconds to turn it on/off. If the device is unresponsive or has minor problems, use the Reset On/Off switch located on the back of the device next to the card reader to reset it back to the factory default.

Troubleshooting Menu and Navigation
*   Problem: Cannot exit the menu or navigate the screen.
*   Solution: Press the <MENU> button to exit the menu display. If the device is in Basic Zone mode, certain menu items (like RAW and RAW JPEG recording quality) will not be displayed. You can also use the < > dial to select menu items or playback images.
*   Note: Menu operation works while the image is being recorded to the CF card (access lamp blinks).

Troubleshooting Audio and Display
*   Problem: No sound or display issues.
*   Solution: Check the volume levels using the ^+ (UP) and ^ (DOWN) buttons. If the display is damaged or difficult to read, cover the device to protect the screen.
*   Note: The device features a 7" TFT LCD Display Screen.

Troubleshooting Physical Damage and Care
*   Problem: Device is damaged or damaged by external factors.
*   Solution: Avoid putting unnecessary pressure on the device or striking it against other objects. Do not drop, crush, bend, or apply excessive force.
*   Prevention: Keep the device covered when not in use. Do not expose it to moisture, extreme temperatures, or harmful conditions. Clean the device with a soft, dry cloth。

操作/说明：
- do not use harsh chemicals.

Troubleshooting Charging and Connectivity
*   Problem: Device is not charging or data transfer fails.
*   Solution: Use the USB Port with a USB cable to charge the device or connect to a computer to transfer contents.
*   Note: The device supports Micro SD/SDHC cards up to 16GB.。

注意事项：
- 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

相关图片：
- Manual23_48、Manual23_49、Manual23_52

question: "When connecting the fax, hwo can I properly finish the procedure?"
ret: "To properly finish the procedure of connecting the fax, follow these steps: 1. Secure the LP Tank: Ensure the LP tank is properly secured onto the grill. 2. Turn Off Controls: Turn all control knobs to the OFF position. 3. Turn Off LP Tank: Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop. 4. Remove Protective Cap: Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve. Always use the cap and strap supplied with the valve. Do not use a POL transport plug (plastic part with external threads) as it will defeat the valve's safety feature. 5. Insert Nipple: Hold the regulator and insert the nipple into the LP tank valve. 6. Hand-Tighten Coupling: Hand-tighten the coupling nut, holding the regulator in a straight line with the LP tank valve so as not to cross the nipple. 7. Tighten to Full Stop: Turn the coupling nut clockwise and tighten to a full stop. The regulator will seal on the back-check feature in the LP tank valve, resulting in some resistance. 8. Complete Connection: An additional one-half to three-quarters turn is required to complete the connection. Tighten by hand only。 do not use tools.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准<PIC>。";["Manual23_16"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- To properly finish the procedure of connecting the fax, follow these steps:

1.  Secure the LP Tank: Ensure the LP tank is properly secured onto the grill.
2.  Turn Off Controls: Turn all control knobs to the OFF position.
3.  Turn Off LP Tank: Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop.
4.  Remove Protective Cap: Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve. Always use the cap and strap supplied with the valve. Do not use a POL transport plug (plastic part with external threads) as it will defeat the valve's safety feature.
5.  Insert Nipple: Hold the regulator and insert the nipple into the LP tank valve.
6.  Hand-Tighten Coupling: Hand-tighten the coupling nut, holding the regulator in a straight line with the LP tank valve so as not to cross the nipple.
7.  Tighten to Full Stop: Turn the coupling nut clockwise and tighten to a full stop. The regulator will seal on the back-check feature in the LP tank valve, resulting in some resistance.
8.  Complete Connection: An additional one-half to three-quarters turn is required to complete the connection. Tighten by hand only。

操作/说明：
- do not use tools.。

- To properly finish the procedure of connecting the fax, follow these steps:

1.  Secure the LP Tank: Ensure the LP tank is properly secured onto the grill.
2.  Turn Off Controls: Turn all control knobs to the OFF position.
3.  Turn Off LP Tank: Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop.
4.  Remove Protective Cap: Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve. Always use the cap and strap supplied with the valve. Do not use a POL transport plug (plastic part with external threads) as it will defeat the valve's safety feature.
5.  Insert Nipple: Hold the regulator and insert the nipple into the LP tank valve.
6.  Hand-Tighten Coupling: Hand-tighten the coupling nut, holding the regulator in a straight line with the LP tank valve so as not to cross the nipple.
7.  Tighten to Full Stop: Turn the coupling nut clockwise and tighten to a full stop. The regulator will seal on the back-check feature in the LP tank valve, resulting in some resistance.
8.  Complete Connection: An additional one-half to three-quarters turn is required to complete the connection. Tighten by hand only。

操作/说明：
- do not use tools.。

question: "I'm about to move this fax. Is there anything I should pay attention to?"
ret: When moving a fax, please follow these steps and pay attention to the following safety and care guidelines: Move the Fax * Seek immediate medical attention if fluid is injected into skin. Injected fluid must be surgically removed within a few hours by a doctor. * Ensure that all hydraulic-fluid hoses and lines are in good condition and all hydraulic connections and fittings are tight before applying pressure to the hydraulic system. * Keep your body and hands away from pinhole leaks or nozzles that eject high-pressure hydraulic fluid. * Use cardboard or paper to find hydraulic leaks. * Safely relieve all pressure in the hydraulic system before performing any work on the hydraulic system. Care for the Fax * Do not drop the memory card or subject it to vibration. Doing so could damage the images recorded on them. * Do not store or use a memory card near anything having a strong magnetic field such as a TV set, speakers, or magnet. Also avoid places prone to having static electricity. Otherwise, the images recorded on the CF card might be lost. * Do not leave memory cards in direct sunlight or near a heat source. Doing so can warp the cards and make them unusable. * Do not bend the card or subject it to any excessive force or physical shock. * Do not store CF cards in hot, dusty, or humid locations. * Do not touch the camera's electrical contacts with your fingers. This is to prevent the contacts from corroding. Corroded contacts can cause camera misoperation. General Safety * Always stop the engines before beaching the boat. * Be sure the boat is in water 90cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat before starting the engines again. * Do not beach the boat on rocky beaches. The hull gelcoat and exposed pump housings can be damaged. * Pay attention to shifts in tides. Beaching at high tide may make it impossible to re-launch the boat if the tide recedes. * Make sure no obstructions, boats, or swimmers are near the beach. * Approach the beach slowly and stop the engines when the water is about 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat. Remember: turning is impossible with the engines stopped. * Get out of the boat and pull the bow up on the beach. * When leaving the beach, push the boat out into water that is at least 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat before starting the engines. Important Note * Do not leave the camera near anything having a strong magnetic field such as a magnet or electric motor. Also avoid using or leaving the camera near anything emitting strong radio waves such as a large antenna. Strong magnetic fields can cause camera misoperation or destroy image data. * Do not leave the camera in excessive heat such as in a car in direct sunlight. High temperatures can cause the camera to malfunction. * The camera contains precision electronic circuitry. Never attempt to disassemble the camera yourself. * Use a blower to blow away dust on the lens, viewfinder, mirror, and focusing screen. Do not use cleaners that contain organic solvents to clean the camera body or lens. For stubborn dirt, take the camera to a professional. Additional Information * In most states, it is required by law to let the person being pulled direct the operator's control of speed and direction with hand signals. * When preparing to pull the wake boarder or water-skier, operate the boat at the slowest possible speed until the boat is well away from the person being pulled and slack in the tow-rope is taken up. Make sure that the rope is not looped around anything. * The operators of boats and other watercraft may not be aware that you are pulling a wake boarder or water-skier. Together with the spotter, pay attention to others around you and cruise at safe speeds. * Be alert to the hazard of the tow-rope handle snapping back at the boat when the wake boarder or water-skier is pulled.。 When moving a fax, please follow these steps and pay attention to the following safety and care guidelines: Move the Fax * Seek immediate medical attention if fluid is injected into skin. raw_answer:
结论：
- When moving a fax, please follow these steps and pay attention to the following safety and care guidelines:

Move the Fax
*   Seek immediate medical attention if fluid is injected into skin. Injected fluid must be surgically removed within a few hours by a doctor.
*   Ensure that all hydraulic-fluid hoses and lines are in good condition and all hydraulic connections and fittings are tight before applying pressure to the hydraulic system.
*   Keep your body and hands away from pinhole leaks or nozzles that eject high-pressure hydraulic fluid.
*   Use cardboard or paper to find hydraulic leaks.
*   Safely relieve all pressure in the hydraulic system before performing any work on the hydraulic system.

Care for the Fax
*   Do not drop the memory card or subject it to vibration. Doing so could damage the images recorded on them.
*   Do not store or use a memory card near anything having a strong magnetic field such as a TV set, speakers, or magnet. Also avoid places prone to having static electricity. Otherwise, the images recorded on the CF card might be lost.
*   Do not leave memory cards in direct sunlight or near a heat source. Doing so can warp the cards and make them unusable.
*   Do not bend the card or subject it to any excessive force or physical shock.
*   Do not store CF cards in hot, dusty, or humid locations.
*   Do not touch the camera's electrical contacts with your fingers. This is to prevent the contacts from corroding. Corroded contacts can cause camera misoperation.

General Safety
*   Always stop the engines before beaching the boat.
*   Be sure the boat is in water 90cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat before starting the engines again.
*   Do not beach the boat on rocky beaches. The hull gelcoat and exposed pump housings can be damaged.
*   Pay attention to shifts in tides. Beaching at high tide may make it impossible to re-launch the boat if the tide recedes.
*   Make sure no obstructions, boats, or swimmers are near the beach.
*   Approach the beach slowly and stop the engines when the water is about 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat. Remember: turning is impossible with the engines stopped.
*   Get out of the boat and pull the bow up on the beach.
*   When leaving the beach, push the boat out into water that is at least 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat before starting the engines.

Important Note
*   Do not leave the camera near anything having a strong magnetic field such as a magnet or electric motor. Also avoid using or leaving the camera near anything emitting strong radio waves such as a large antenna. Strong magnetic fields can cause camera misoperation or destroy image data.
*   Do not leave the camera in excessive heat such as in a car in direct sunlight. High temperatures can cause the camera to malfunction.
*   The camera contains precision electronic circuitry. Never attempt to disassemble the camera yourself.
*   Use a blower to blow away dust on the lens, viewfinder, mirror, and focusing screen. Do not use cleaners that contain organic solvents to clean the camera body or lens. For stubborn dirt, take the camera to a professional.

Additional Information
*   In most states, it is required by law to let the person being pulled direct the operator's control of speed and direction with hand signals.
*   When preparing to pull the wake boarder or water-skier, operate the boat at the slowest possible speed until the boat is well away from the person being pulled and slack in the tow-rope is taken up. Make sure that the rope is not looped around anything.
*   The operators of boats and other watercraft may not be aware that you are pulling a wake boarder or water-skier. Together with the spotter, pay attention to others around you and cruise at safe speeds.
*   Be alert to the hazard of the tow-rope handle snapping back at the boat when the wake boarder or water-skier is pulled.。

question: "When using the grill, how to connect regulator to the LP Tank?"
ret: 1. Turn all control knobs to the OFF position. 2. Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop. 3. Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve and always use the cap and strap supplied with the valve. Do not use a POL transport plug. 4. Hold the regulator and insert the nipple into the LP tank valve. 5. Hand-tighten the coupling nut while holding the regulator in a straight line with the LP tank valve to ensure the nipple is centered. 6. Turn the coupling nut clockwise and tighten to a full stop. An additional one-half to three-quarters of a turn is required to complete the connection. 7. Tighten by hand only。 do not use tools. Important Notes: * Do not insert any tool or foreign objects into the valve outlet or safety relief valve, as this may damage the valve and cause a leak. * If a leak is detected, stop and call the fire department. If you cannot stop a gas leak, immediately close the LP tank valve and call the LP gas supplier or your fire department. * If you cannot complete the connection, disconnect the regulator and repeat steps 5 and 6. If you are still unable to complete the connection, do not use this regulator. * Always keep new and exchanged LP tanks in an upright position during use, transit, or storage. * Before connecting to a grill, leak test new and exchanged LP tanks. * Store the LP tank outdoors in a well-ventilated space out of reach of children. Do not store it in enclosed spaces like garages or covered patios.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- 1. Turn all control knobs to the OFF position.
2. Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop.
3. Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve and always use the cap and strap supplied with the valve. Do not use a POL transport plug.
4. Hold the regulator and insert the nipple into the LP tank valve.
5. Hand-tighten the coupling nut while holding the regulator in a straight line with the LP tank valve to ensure the nipple is centered.
6. Turn the coupling nut clockwise and tighten to a full stop. An additional one-half to three-quarters of a turn is required to complete the connection.
7. Tighten by hand only。

操作/说明：
- do not use tools.

Important Notes:
*   Do not insert any tool or foreign objects into the valve outlet or safety relief valve, as this may damage the valve and cause a leak.
*   If a leak is detected, stop and call the fire department. If you cannot stop a gas leak, immediately close the LP tank valve and call the LP gas supplier or your fire department.
*   If you cannot complete the connection, disconnect the regulator and repeat steps 5 and 6. If you are still unable to complete the connection, do not use this regulator.
*   Always keep new and exchanged LP tanks in an upright position during use, transit, or storage.
*   Before connecting to a grill, leak test new and exchanged LP tanks.
*   Store the LP tank outdoors in a well-ventilated space out of reach of children. Do not store it in enclosed spaces like garages or covered patios.。

注意事项：
- 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

相关图片：
- 无

question: "To engure my safety when using the grill, how to do leak testing on valves, hose and regulatol?"
ret: To ensure your safety when using the grill, follow these steps for leak testing the valves, hose, and regulator: 1. Turn all grill control knobs to OFF. 2. Be sure the regulator is tightly connected to the LP tank. 3. Completely open the LP tank valve by turning the hand wheel counterclockwise. If you hear a rushing sound, turn the gas off immediately. There is a major leak at the connection。 correct it before proceeding by calling Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME. 4. Brush soapy solution on to areas where bubbles are shown in the picture below. 5. If "growing" bubbles appear, there is a leak. Close the LP tank valve immediately and retighten the connections. If leaks cannot be stopped, do not try to repair them。 call Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME. 6. Always close the LP tank valve after performing the leak test by turning the hand wheel clockwise. Important Safety Notes: Never attempt to attach this grill to the self-contained LP gas system of a camper trailer or motor home. Do not use the grill until it has been leak-tested. Always keep the LP cylinder in an upright position during use, transport, and storage. Ensure the cylinder valve faces the front of the grill once the tank is attached to prevent gas hose damage and fire risks.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- To ensure your safety when using the grill, follow these steps for leak testing the valves, hose, and regulator:

1. Turn all grill control knobs to OFF.
2. Be sure the regulator is tightly connected to the LP tank.
3. Completely open the LP tank valve by turning the hand wheel counterclockwise. If you hear a rushing sound, turn the gas off immediately. There is a major leak at the connection。

操作/说明：
- correct it before proceeding by calling Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME.
4. Brush soapy solution on to areas where bubbles are shown in the picture below.
5. If "growing" bubbles appear, there is a leak. Close the LP tank valve immediately and retighten the connections. If leaks cannot be stopped, do not try to repair them。
- call Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME.
6. Always close the LP tank valve after performing the leak test by turning the hand wheel clockwise.

Important Safety Notes:
- Never attempt to attach this grill to the self-contained LP gas system of a camper trailer or motor home.
- Do not use the grill until it has been leak-tested.
- Always keep the LP cylinder in an upright position during use, transport, and storage.
- Ensure the cylinder valve faces the front of the grill once the tank is attached to prevent gas hose damage and fire risks.。

注意事项：

结论：
- To ensure your safety when using the grill, follow these steps for leak testing the valves, hose, and regulator:

1. Turn all grill control knobs to OFF.
2. Be sure the regulator is tightly connected to the LP tank.
3. Completely open the LP tank valve by turning the hand wheel counterclockwise. If you hear a rushing sound, turn the gas off immediately. There is a major leak at the connection。

操作/说明：
- correct it before proceeding by calling Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME.
4. Brush soapy solution on to areas where bubbles are shown in the picture below.
5. If "growing" bubbles appear, there is a leak. Close the LP tank valve immediately and retighten the connections. If leaks cannot be stopped, do not try to repair them。
- call Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME.
6. Always close the LP tank valve after performing the leak test by turning the hand wheel clockwise.

Important Safety Notes:
- Never attempt to attach this grill to the self-contained LP gas system of a camper trailer or motor home.
- Do not use the grill until it has been leak-tested.
- Always keep the LP cylinder in an upright position during use, transport, and storage.
- Ensure the cylinder valve faces the front of the grill once the tank is attached to prevent gas hose damage and fire risks.。

question: "What should I pay attention to on ablout indirect cooking using the grill?"
ret: When using the grill for indirect cooking, you should pay attention to the following: 1. Always cook with the lid closed: This helps maintain consistent heat and prevents moisture loss. Due to weather conditions, cooking times may vary, so you may need to increase the temperature setting in cold or windy conditions to ensure sufficient cooking temperatures. 2. Avoid direct flames: Indirect cooking uses selected burners to circulate gentle heat, which reduces flare-ups when cooking extra fatty cuts by preventing direct ignition of dripping fats and juices. 3. Monitor fuel consumption: This method consumes less fuel compared to direct cooking methods, making it ideal for smaller meals or foods. 4. Ensure proper ventilation: When storing the grill outdoors, it must be stored in a well-ventilated space and out of reach of children. If the LP tank is connected, it should be stored indoors only if the tank is turned off and disconnected. 5. Prevent debris blockage: Small pebbles, sand, seaweed, and other debris can be sucked into jet intakes, impairing or damaging the impellers. Always stop the engines before beaching the boat and ensure the water is at least 90 cm (3 ft) deep before restarting. 6. Clean grates regularly: Keep cooking grates clean to maintain optimal performance and safety.。 When using the grill for indirect cooking, you should pay attention to the following: 1. question: "Can you share me some safety tips using the grill?"
ret: Here are some essential safety tips for using your grill: 1. Pre-Use Checks * Check Tightness: Before opening the LP tank valve, check the coupling nut for tightness. * Turn Off Controls: If the grill is not in use, turn off all control knobs and the LP tank valve. * Never Move While Hot: Never move the grill while it is in operation or still hot. * Use Long-Handled Tools: Use long-handled barbecue utensils and oven mitts to avoid burns and splatters. * Inspect for Leaks: If grease or hot material drips from the grill onto the valve, hose, or regulator, turn off the gas supply immediately, determine the cause, correct it, clean and inspect the hose and regulator, then perform a leak test. 2. Installation and Code Compliance * Propane Only: Use the grill only with LP (propane) gas and the regulator/valve assembly supplied. * Follow Codes: Grill installation must conform with local codes or applicable standards such as the National Fuel Gas Code (ANSI Z223.1/NFPA 54), Natural Gas and Propane Installation Code (CSA B149.2), or the Standard for Recreational Vehicles (ANSI A119.2). * Electrical Safety: Any electrical accessories (like a rotisserie) must be electrically grounded in accordance with local codes. * Keep Cords Away: Keep electrical cords and fuel supply hoses away from any hot surfaces. * Do Not Modify: Do not modify the grill for use in any other location, as this will result in a safety hazard. 3. Operational Safety * Grease Tray: Install the grease tray into the opening in the back panel. Do not remove it until the grill has completely cooled. * Avoid Hot Surfaces: Keep any electrical cords and fuel hoses away from hot surfaces. * Gas Supply: If the regulator frosts, turn off the grill and LP tank valve immediately. This indicates a problem with the tank and should not be used. * Leak Test: If you notice grease dripping from the grill onto the valve, hose, or regulator, perform a leak test. The regulator may make a humming or whistling noise during operation, which does not affect safety or use. * Troubleshooting: If you have a grill problem, refer to the "Troubleshooting Section." 4. Special Warnings * Vacuum Safety: Do not expose the electronics, battery, or integrated dock charger. Ensure the voltage rating matches the standard outlet voltage. * Vacuum Usage: Do not use the vacuum in rooms with balconies without a physical barrier to prevent access. Do not use it by persons with reduced physical, sensory, or mental capabilities without supervision. * Snowmobiles: When riding a snowmobile, know and use the safety information provided (severe injury or death may result if ignored). <PIC> [Image of a grill with safety tips listed] </PIC>。 Here are some essential safety tips for using your grill: 1. raw_answer:
结论：
- Here are some essential safety tips for using your grill:

1. Pre-Use Checks
*   Check Tightness: Before opening the LP tank valve, check the coupling nut for tightness.
*   Turn Off Controls: If the grill is not in use, turn off all control knobs and the LP tank valve.
*   Never Move While Hot: Never move the grill while it is in operation or still hot.
*   Use Long-Handled Tools: Use long-handled barbecue utensils and oven mitts to avoid burns and splatters.
*   Inspect for Leaks: If grease or hot material drips from the grill onto the valve, hose, or regulator, turn off the gas supply immediately, determine the cause, correct it, clean and inspect the hose and regulator, then perform a leak test.

2. Installation and Code Compliance
*   Propane Only: Use the grill only with LP (propane) gas and the regulator/valve assembly supplied.
*   Follow Codes: Grill installation must conform with local codes or applicable standards such as the National Fuel Gas Code (ANSI Z223.1/NFPA 54), Natural Gas and Propane Installation Code (CSA B149.2), or the Standard for Recreational Vehicles (ANSI A119.2).
*   Electrical Safety: Any electrical accessories (like a rotisserie) must be electrically grounded in accordance with local codes.
*   Keep Cords Away: Keep electrical cords and fuel supply hoses away from any hot surfaces.
*   Do Not Modify: Do not modify the grill for use in any other location, as this will result in a safety hazard.

3. Operational Safety
*   Grease Tray: Install the grease tray into the opening in the back panel. Do not remove it until the grill has completely cooled.
*   Avoid Hot Surfaces: Keep any electrical cords and fuel hoses away from hot surfaces.
*   Gas Supply: If the regulator frosts, turn off the grill and LP tank valve immediately. This indicates a problem with the tank and should not be used.
*   Leak Test: If you notice grease dripping from the grill onto the valve, hose, or regulator, perform a leak test. The regulator may make a humming or whistling noise during operation, which does not affect safety or use.
*   Troubleshooting: If you have a grill problem, refer to the "Troubleshooting Section."

4. Special Warnings
*   Vacuum Safety: Do not expose the electronics, battery, or integrated dock charger. Ensure the voltage rating matches the standard outlet voltage.
*   Vacuum Usage: Do not use the vacuum in rooms with balconies without a physical barrier to prevent access. Do not use it by persons with reduced physical, sensory, or mental capabilities without supervision.
*   Snowmobiles: When riding a snowmobile, know and use the safety information provided (severe injury or death may result if ignored).

raw_answer:
问题1：What are identification numbers of my jetski?
结论：
- Your jetski identification numbers are as follows:

1. Primary Identification (PRI-ID) Number: Record this number on the vehicle.
2. Hull Identification Number (HIN): Record this number on the vehicle.
3. Engine Serial Number: Record this number on the vehicle.

How to obtain these numbers:
- PRI-ID Number: This is typically found on the vehicle's identification plate or label.
- HIN: This is usually located on the hull or frame of the jetski.
- Engine Serial Number: This can be found on the engine label or plate.

Important Notes:
- Keep these numbers safe: Store them in a separate place in case your watercraft is stolen.
- Ordering parts: When ordering spare parts from a jetski dealer, record these numbers in the spaces provided for assistance.
- File Numbering: If you are using a CF card to store images, note that the file numbering resets to XxX-0001 each time the card is replaced.

Related Information:
- For detailed information, please refer to the manual pages provided in parentheses.
- If you are using a CB-5L camera, ensure the battery is fully charged before use, as the red lamp indicates the charging status.

<PIC>。

问题2：I don't understand that.
结论：
- Your jetski has three main identification numbers that are stamped on labels attached to different parts of the boat. Here is how to find and record them:

1.  Primary Identification (PRI-ID) Number:
    *   Location: Stamped on a label inside the engine compartment.
    *   Action: Record this number in the provided space. Keep it safe in case your watercraft is stolen.
    *   *(See page 56 for engine hood opening and closing procedures.)*

2.  Hull Identification Number (HIN):
    *   Location: Stamped on the hull of the boat.
    *   Action: Record this number in the provided space. Keep it safe in case your watercraft is stolen.

3.  Engine Serial Number:
    *   Location: Stamped on a label attached to each engine unit.
    *   Action: Record this number in the provided space. Keep it safe in case your watercraft is stolen.

Important Note:
*   Model and Serial Numbers: These are found on the back of this unit (the product packaging).
*   Proof of Purchase: You should record the requested information here and retain your purchase receipt as your permanent record of your purchase.
*   Safety: Never place the television receiver on a bed, sofa, rug, or similar surface. Do not place it in a built-in installation (like a bookcase) unless proper ventilation is provided. Do not operate it near speakers or big metallic furniture to avoid magnetic interference.。

question: "I've heard that my jetski has cruising limitations. Please introduce them."
ret: Cruising limitations refer to specific restrictions and safety guidelines that apply when operating your boat. These are designed to prevent accidents and ensure the safety of the operator and others on the water. Key Limitations Include: * Visibility and Awareness: You must constantly scan for people, objects, and other watercraft. Be alert for conditions that limit your visibility or block your vision of others. * Speed and Distance: Operate defensively at safe speeds and keep a safe distance away from people, objects, and other watercraft. Do not follow directly behind watercraft or other boats. * Maneuvering: Avoid sharp turns or other maneuvers that make it hard for others to avoid you or understand where you are going. * Water Conditions: Do not operate the watercraft in rough water, bad weather, or when visibility is poor. * Depth Restrictions: Never operate in water that is less than 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat, as this increases the risk of hitting a submerged object. * Weather Awareness: Take note of weather forecasts and prevailing weather conditions before setting out. * Float Plan: Leave a "float plan" with a responsible person on shore. This includes telling them where you plan to go, when you plan to arrive, and providing a description of your boat. * Navigation Rules: Follow navigation rules and state and local laws that apply to your boat. Additional Recommendations: * Avoid aggressive maneuvers to reduce the risk of loss of control, ejection, and collision. * Do not go near others to spray or splash them with water. * Avoid areas with submerged objects or shallow water. * If you are operating at night, be extra cautious and follow the specific safety information provided in the "Night operation" section.。 Cruising limitations refer to specific restrictions and safety guidelines that apply when operating your boat. These are designed to prevent accidents and ensure the safety of the operator and others on the water. Be alert for conditions that limit your visibility or block your vision of others. * Speed and Distance: Operate defensively at safe speeds and keep a safe distance away from people, objects, and other watercraft. Do not follow directly behind watercraft or other boats. * Maneuvering: Avoid sharp turns or other maneuvers that make it hard for others to avoid you or understand where you are going. * Water Conditions: Do not operate the watercraft in rough water, bad weather, or when visibility is poor. * Depth Restrictions: Never operate in water that is less than 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat, as this increases the risk of hitting a submerged object. * Weather Awareness: Take note of weather forecasts and prevailing weather conditions before setting out. * Float Plan: Leave a "float plan" with a responsible person on shore. This includes telling them where you plan to go, when you plan to arrive, and providing a description of your boat. * Navigation Rules: Follow navigation rules and state and local laws that apply to your boat. Additional Recommendations: * Avoid aggressive maneuvers to reduce the risk of loss of control, ejection, and collision. * Do not go near others to spray or splash them with water. * Avoid areas with submerged objects or shallow water. * If you are operating at night, be extra cautious and follow the specific safety information provided in the "Night operation" section.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

question: "I've heard that my jetski has cruising limitations. Please introduce them."
ret: Cruising limitations refer to specific restrictions and safety guidelines designed to prevent accidents, ensure the safety of passengers and other users, and protect the integrity of the boat and its components. Based on the provided references, here are the key limitations: 1. Visibility and Awareness: You must constantly scan for people, objects, and other watercraft. Be alert for conditions that limit your visibility or block your vision of others. 2. Speed and Distance: Operate defensively at safe speeds and keep a safe distance away from people, objects, and other watercraft. Do not follow directly behind watercraft or other boats. 3. Avoidance of Hazards: Do not go near others to spray or splash them with water. Avoid sharp turns or other maneuvers that make it hard for others to avoid you or understand where you are going. 4. Water Conditions: Avoid areas with submerged objects or shallow water. Do not operate the watercraft in rough water, bad weather, or when visibility is poor. 5. Depth Restrictions: Never operate in water that is less than 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat, as this increases the chance of hitting a submerged object. 6. Weather Awareness: Take note of weather forecasts and the prevailing weather conditions before setting out in your boat. Be alert to the possibility of adverse weather. 7. Float Plan: Leave a "float plan" with a responsible person on shore. Tell them where you plan to go and when you plan to arrive, and provide a description of your boat. 结论：
- Cruising limitations refer to specific restrictions and safety guidelines designed to prevent accidents, ensure the safety of passengers and other users, and protect the integrity of the boat and its components. Based on the provided references, here are the key limitations:

1.  Visibility and Awareness: You must constantly scan for people, objects, and other watercraft. Be alert for conditions that limit your visibility or block your vision of others.
2.  Speed and Distance: Operate defensively at safe speeds and keep a safe distance away from people, objects, and other watercraft. Do not follow directly behind watercraft or other boats.
3.  Avoidance of Hazards: Do not go near others to spray or splash them with water. Avoid sharp turns or other maneuvers that make it hard for others to avoid you or understand where you are going.
4.  Water Conditions: Avoid areas with submerged objects or shallow water. Do not operate the watercraft in rough water, bad weather, or when visibility is poor.
5.  Depth Restrictions: Never operate in water that is less than 90 cm (3 ft) deep from the bottom of the boat, as this increases the chance of hitting a submerged object.
6.  Weather Awareness: Take note of weather forecasts and the prevailing weather conditions before setting out in your boat. Be alert to the possibility of adverse weather.
7.  Float Plan: Leave a "float plan" with a responsible person on shore. Tell them where you plan to go and when you plan to arrive, and provide a description of your boat. Advise this person if your plans change and also when you arrive to prevent false alarms.

question: "What are the operation requirements if I want to use my jetski?"
ret: "All riders must wear a Coast Guard approved personal flotation device (PFD) that is suitable for personal watercraft use, along with protective clothing. Severe internal injuries can occur if water is forced into body cavities。 normal swimwear does not provide adequate protection, so a wetsuit bottom or thick, tightly woven, sturdy, and snug-fitting apparel (such as denim) is required. Eye protection is recommended to keep wind, water, and glare out of your eyes, with restraining straps designed to float if eyewear falls in the water. Footwear and gloves are also recommended. Operational requirements include: 1. Scan constantly for swimmers and stay away from swimming areas, as swimmers are hard to see and you could accidentally hit someone in the water. 2. Avoid being hit by another boat。 you should always take responsibility to watch for traffic, and if other boaters do not see you or maneuver more quickly than expected, you risk a collision. 3. Maintain a safe distance from other boats and watercraft, and also watch for ski ropes or fishing lines. 4. Obey the "Rules of the road" and be sure to check behind you before making a turn. Additionally, if you use the self-timer, you can use it in any Basic Zone mode or Creative Zone mode to be in the picture.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准<PIC><PIC>。";["Manual23_46", "Manual23_47"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- All riders must wear a Coast Guard approved personal flotation device (PFD) that is suitable for personal watercraft use, along with protective clothing. Severe internal injuries can occur if water is forced into body cavities。

操作/说明：
- normal swimwear does not provide adequate protection, so a wetsuit bottom or thick, tightly woven, sturdy, and snug-fitting apparel (such as denim) is required. Eye protection is recommended to keep wind, water, and glare out of your eyes, with restraining straps designed to float if eyewear falls in the water. Footwear and gloves are also recommended.

Operational requirements include:
1. Scan constantly for swimmers and stay away from swimming areas, as swimmers are hard to see and you could accidentally hit someone in the water.
2. Avoid being hit by another boat。
- you should always take responsibility to watch for traffic, and if other boaters do not see you or maneuver more quickly than expected, you risk a collision.
3. Maintain a safe distance from other boats and watercraft, and also watch for ski ropes or fishing lines.
4. Obey the "Rules of the road" and be sure to check behind you before making a turn.

Additionally, if you use the self-timer, you can use it in any Basic Zone mode or Creative Zone mode to be in the picture.。

注意事项：
- 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

相关图片：
- Manual23_46、Manual23_47

Operational requirements include:

question: "What kinds of watercraft characteristics does my jetski have?"
ret: Your jetski has the following characteristics: Jet Thrust: The jet thrust turns the watercraft. Releasing the throttle completely produces only minimum thrust. Steering Behavior: If you are traveling at speeds above trolling, you will have rapidly decreasing ability to steer without throttle. You may still have some turning ability immediately after releasing the throttle, but once the engine slows down, the watercraft will no longer respond to handlebar input until you open the throttle again or reach a trolling speed. Turning Practice: Practice turning in an open area without obstructions until you have a good feel for this maneuver. Safety Precautions: Stop the engine and remove the clip (extcircled 3) from the engine shut-off switch (extcircled 4) before removing any debris or weeds that may have collected around the jet intake. <PIC> [Image of a jetski with jet thrust and throttle controls] </PIC> Limitations on Operation: Operator Age: A minimum operator age of 16 years old is recommended. Supervision: Adults must supervise use by minors. Training: Know the operator age and training requirements for your state. A boating safety course is recommended and may be required in your state. Capacity: This watercraft is designed to carry the operator and one passenger. Never exceed the maximum load limit or allow more than 2 persons to ride the watercraft at one time. Skill Requirement: Do not operate the watercraft with a passenger on board until you have considerable practice and experience riding alone. Operating the watercraft with a passenger requires more skill. <PIC> [Image of a jetski with operator and passenger capacity] </PIC> Legal Status: The jetski is legally considered a powerboat and must be operated in accordance with the rules and regulations governing the waterway on which it is used. <PIC> [Image of a powerboat and watercraft rules] </PIC> Education and Training: The Online Boating Safety Course is available through the watercraft section of the jetski-motor.com website. It is a free, 50-question learning course. Upon successful completion of 80 percent or better, the user can request a certificate of completion by mail or download one immediately. This course is approved by the National Association of State Boating Law Administrators (NASBLA) and recognized by the United States Coast Guard. <PIC> [Image of the Online Boating Safety Course] </PIC>。 Your jetski has the following characteristics: Jet Thrust: The jet thrust turns the watercraft. raw_answer:
结论：
- Your jetski has the following characteristics:

- Jet Thrust: The jet thrust turns the watercraft. Releasing the throttle completely produces only minimum thrust.
- Steering Behavior: If you are traveling at speeds above trolling, you will have rapidly decreasing ability to steer without throttle. You may still have some turning ability immediately after releasing the throttle, but once the engine slows down, the watercraft will no longer respond to handlebar input until you open the throttle again or reach a trolling speed.
- Turning Practice: Practice turning in an open area without obstructions until you have a good feel for this maneuver.
- Safety Precautions: Stop the engine and remove the clip (extcircled 3) from the engine shut-off switch (extcircled 4) before removing any debris or weeds that may have collected around the jet intake.

<PIC>
[Image of a jetski with jet thrust and throttle controls]
</PIC>

Limitations on Operation:
- Operator Age: A minimum operator age of 16 years old is recommended.
- Supervision: Adults must supervise use by minors.
- Training: Know the operator age and training requirements for your state. A boating safety course is recommended and may be required in your state.
- Capacity: This watercraft is designed to carry the operator and one passenger. Never exceed the maximum load limit or allow more than 2 persons to ride the watercraft at one time.
- Skill Requirement: Do not operate the watercraft with a passenger on board until you have considerable practice and experience riding alone. Operating the watercraft with a passenger requires more skill.

<PIC>
[Image of a jetski with operator and passenger capacity]
</PIC>

Legal Status:
- The jetski is legally considered a powerboat and must be operated in accordance with the rules and regulations governing the waterway on which it is used.

<PIC>

</PIC>

- The Online Boating Safety Course is available through the watercraft section of the jetski-motor.com website. It is a free, 50-question learning course. Upon successful completion of 80 percent or better, the user can request a certificate of completion by mail or download one immediately.
- This course is approved by the National Association of State Boating Law Administrators (NASBLA) and recognized by the United States Coast Guard.

<PIC>
[Image of the Online Boating Safety Course]
</PIC>。

question: "When encountering vessels driving my jetski, what rules should I obey?"
ret: When encountering vessels driving your jetski, you should obey the following rules: 1. Legal Classification: As per the rules of the road, a jetski watercraft is legally considered a powerboat. 2. Compliance with Local Rules: You must operate your watercraft in accordance with the rules and regulations governing the specific waterway you are on. While these rules are generally the same as the International Rules of the Road, you should consult your local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary or Department of Motor Vehicles for the complete set of rules governing the waters in which you will be operating. 3. Right-of-Way: * Stand-on Vessel: The vessel that is not passing or being passed (the one you are approaching or crossing) has the right-of-way. * Give-Way Vessel: The vessel that is passing or being passed (the one you are overtaking) is the "give-way" or "burdened" vessel and must yield to the stand-on vessel. <PIC> [Image ID: 1] </PIC> Important Safety Precautions: * Do not leave cords near heat sources: This can deform the cord, melt the insulation, or cause a fire or electrical shock. * Do not fire the flash: * Near someone driving a car (it may cause an accident). * Near a person's eyes (it may impair vision). * When photographing an infant, keep at least 1 meter away. * Do not use equipment where flammable gas is present: This is to prevent an explosion or fire.。 When encountering vessels driving your jetski, you should obey the following rules: 1. Compliance with Local Rules: You must operate your watercraft in accordance with the rules and regulations governing the specific waterway you are on. While these rules are generally the same as the International Rules of the Road, you should consult your local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary or Department of Motor Vehicles for the complete set of rules governing the waters in which you will be operating. 3. Right-of-Way: * Stand-on Vessel: The vessel that is not passing or being passed (the one you are approaching or crossing) has the right-of-way. * Give-Way Vessel: The vessel that is passing or being passed (the one you are overtaking) is the "give-way" or "burdened" vessel and must yield to the stand-on vessel. <PIC> [Image ID: 1] </PIC> Important Safety Precautions: * Do not leave cords near heat sources: This can deform the cord, melt the insulation, or cause a fire or electrical shock. * Do not fire the flash: * Near someone driving a car (it may cause an accident). * Near a person's eyes (it may impair vision). * When photographing an infant, keep at least 1 meter away. * Do not use equipment where flammable gas is present: This is to prevent an explosion or fire.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- When encountering vessels driving your jetski, you should obey the following rules:

1.  Legal Classification: As per the rules of the road, a jetski watercraft is legally considered a powerboat.
2.  Compliance with Local Rules: You must operate your watercraft in accordance with the rules and regulations governing the specific waterway you are on. While these rules are generally the same as the International Rules of the Road, you should consult your local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary or Department of Motor Vehicles for the complete set of rules governing the waters in which you will be operating.
3.  Right-of-Way:
    *   Stand-on Vessel: The vessel that is not passing or being passed (the one you are approaching or crossing) has the right-of-way.
    *   Give-Way Vessel: The vessel that is passing or being passed (the one you are overtaking) is the "give-way" or "burdened" vessel and must yield to the stand-on vessel.

*   Do not leave cords near heat sources: This can deform the cord, melt the insulation, or cause a fire or electrical shock.

    *   Near a person's eyes (it may impair vision).
    *   When photographing an infant, keep at least 1 meter away.
*   Do not use equipment where flammable gas is present: This is to prevent an explosion or fire.。

question: "There are two kinds of levers on the jetski, how to manipulate them?"
ret: The jetski has two types of levers: shift gates and jet thrust nozzles. Their manipulation depends on the boat's speed and the desired action (steering or propulsion): ### 1. Shift Gates * Function: Control the direction of thrust. * Initial Position: When the remote control levers are initially moved to the forward position, the shift gates are lifted slightly from the neutral position. This directs jet thrust downward at an angle, providing good handling response when steering. * Forward Position: When the levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates are lifted all the way up. In this position, all jet thrust is directed rearward, which moves the boat forward. ### 2. Jet Thrust Nozzles * Function: Provide propulsion (thrust). * Initial Position: When the remote control levers are initially moved to the forward position, the jet thrust nozzles are in a neutral position. * Forward Position: When the levers are moved farther forward, the jet thrust nozzles are also lifted all the way up. In this position, all jet thrust is directed rearward, moving the boat forward. ### Summary of Manipulation To move the boat forward: 1. Move the remote control levers to the forward position. 2. This action lifts the shift gates and jet thrust nozzles to the up (forward) position. 3. Both components direct thrust rearward to propel the boat forward. To steer without significant thrust (at low engine speeds): 1. Move the remote control levers to the forward position. 2. The shift gates lift slightly from the neutral position, directing thrust downward at an angle. 3. This allows the boat to respond to steering wheel input even with less available thrust. Note: Pulling the remote control levers back to the idle or neutral position produces only minimum thrust and significantly reduces steering ability.。 The jetski has two types of levers: shift gates and jet thrust nozzles. Their manipulation depends on the boat's speed and the desired action (steering or propulsion): ### 1. Shift Gates * Function: Control the direction of thrust. * Initial Position: When the remote control levers are initially moved to the forward position, the shift gates are lifted slightly from the neutral position. This directs jet thrust downward at an angle, providing good handling response when steering. * Forward Position: When the levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates are lifted all the way up. In this position, all jet thrust is directed rearward, which moves the boat forward. ### 2. Jet Thrust Nozzles * Function: Provide propulsion (thrust). * Initial Position: When the remote control levers are initially moved to the forward position, the jet thrust nozzles are in a neutral position. * Forward Position: When the levers are moved farther forward, the jet thrust nozzles are also lifted all the way up. In this position, all jet thrust is directed rearward, moving the boat forward. ### Summary of Manipulation To move the boat forward: 1. Move the remote control levers to the forward position. 2. This action lifts the shift gates and jet thrust nozzles to the up (forward) position. 3. Both components direct thrust rearward to propel the boat forward. The shift gates lift slightly from the neutral position, directing thrust downward at an angle. 3. This allows the boat to respond to steering wheel input even with less available thrust. raw_answer:
结论：
- The jetski has two types of levers: shift gates and jet thrust nozzles. Their manipulation depends on the boat's speed and the desired action (steering or propulsion):

### 1. Shift Gates
*   Function: Control the direction of thrust.
*   Initial Position: When the remote control levers are initially moved to the forward position, the shift gates are lifted slightly from the neutral position. This directs jet thrust downward at an angle, providing good handling response when steering.
*   Forward Position: When the levers are moved farther forward, the shift gates are lifted all the way up. In this position, all jet thrust is directed rearward, which moves the boat forward.

### 2. Jet Thrust Nozzles
*   Function: Provide propulsion (thrust).
*   Initial Position: When the remote control levers are initially moved to the forward position, the jet thrust nozzles are in a neutral position.
*   Forward Position: When the levers are moved farther forward, the jet thrust nozzles are also lifted all the way up. In this position, all jet thrust is directed rearward, moving the boat forward.

### Summary of Manipulation
To move the boat forward:
1.  Move the remote control levers to the forward position.
2.  This action lifts the shift gates and jet thrust nozzles to the up (forward) position.
3.  Both components direct thrust rearward to propel the boat forward.

To steer without significant thrust (at low engine speeds):
1.  Move the remote control levers to the forward position.
2.  The shift gates lift slightly from the neutral position, directing thrust downward at an angle.
3.  This allows the boat to respond to steering wheel input even with less available thrust.

Note: Pulling the remote control levers back to the idle or neutral position produces only minimum thrust and significantly reduces steering ability.。

question: "What information should I obtain about Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector when using my jetski"
ret: "To obtain information about the Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector when using your jetski, you should obtain the following details: 1. Location: The QSTS selector is located at the left handlebar grip. 2. Function: It is used to adjust the trim angle of the watercraft by changing the angle of the jet thrust nozzle vertically. 3. Operating Principle: Operating the selector changes the trim angle, which in turn changes the direction of the jet thrust. 4. Available Positions: There are 5 positions in total: * 2 positions for bow down (indicated by the symbols `\\circledast` and `\\circleddash`). * 1 position for neutral (indicated by `\\Lsh`). * 2 positions for bow up (indicated by `\\copyright` and `.`). 5. raw_answer:
结论：
- To obtain information about the Quick Shift Trim System (QSTS) selector when using your jetski, you should obtain the following details:

1.  Location: The QSTS selector is located at the left handlebar grip.
2.  Function: It is used to adjust the trim angle of the watercraft by changing the angle of the jet thrust nozzle vertically.
3.  Operating Principle: Operating the selector changes the trim angle, which in turn changes the direction of the jet thrust.
4.  Available Positions: There are 5 positions in total:
    *   2 positions for bow down (indicated by the symbols `\\circledast` and `\\circleddash`).
    *   1 position for neutral (indicated by `\\Lsh`).
    *   2 positions for bow up (indicated by `\\copyright` and `.`).
5.  Safety Warning: The manual states that because jetski has a policy of continuing product improvement, this manual may not be exactly as described. Specifications are subject to change without notice.

question: "What do fuel meter and hour meter on my jetski do?"
ret: Fuel meter: The fuel meter is provided for convenient fuel level checking while riding. It has eight segments that show the amount of fuel remaining in the fuel tank. The indication of the segments differs on your operating condition。 use the meter as a reference. Hour meter: The hour meter is provided to make it easy to follow the maintenance schedule. It shows the hours of engine operation that have elapsed since the watercraft was new. It operates when the engine is running. Multifunction information center: This meter contains the following functions for help and convenience of operation: * Tachometer (Speedometer) * Fuel meter * Hour meter * Warning light * Oil warning indicator * Engine overheat warning indicator * Fuel warning indicator * Reset button Safety-Interlock Indicators: There are symbols on the hour meter that indicate with a black triangle that the interlock component is positioned correctly.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。
raw_answer:
结论：
- Fuel meter: The fuel meter is provided for convenient fuel level checking while riding. It has eight segments that show the amount of fuel remaining in the fuel tank. The indication of the segments differs on your operating condition。

操作/说明：
- use the meter as a reference.

Hour meter: The hour meter is provided to make it easy to follow the maintenance schedule. It shows the hours of engine operation that have elapsed since the watercraft was new. It operates when the engine is running.

Multifunction information center: This meter contains the following functions for help and convenience of operation:
*   Tachometer (Speedometer)
*   Fuel meter
*   Hour meter
*   Warning light
*   Oil warning indicator
*   Engine overheat warning indicator
*   Fuel warning indicator
*   Reset button

Safety-Interlock Indicators: There are symbols on the hour meter that indicate with a black triangle that the interlock component is positioned correctly.。

question: "Inspecting fuel filter and fuel tank of my jetski is important. How to do that?"
ret: "Inspecting your jet ski's fuel filter and fuel tank is crucial for safety and engine performance. Here is how to perform the inspection: 1. Safety First: Before inspecting the fuel tank, ensure the engine is off, the key is removed, and the machine has cooled down. Park the jet ski on a level surface and engage the parking brake. 2. Inspect the Fuel Filter: * Locate the fuel filter, which is found near the engine on the left front side. * Check the filter for any signs of dirt, debris, or damage. * Important: Never install a dirty filter after removing it from the fuel line. If the filter is dirty, have a Yamaha dealer replace it. * Ensure the flow-direction arrow on the replacement filter points toward the engine. 3. Inspect the Fuel Tank: * Visually check the exterior of the fuel tank for leaks, cracks, or corrosion. * Inspect the fuel lines connected to the tank for any signs of damage or leaks. * Ensure the fuel tank cap is securely tightened. 4. Check for Water: * Look inside the fuel tank (if accessible) or near the filter for water accumulation. Water in the fuel filter can cause engine damage. * If water is found in the filter, it must be replaced. Note: Do not attempt to replace the fuel filter yourself if you are not experienced。 having a Yamaha dealer perform the replacement is recommended to ensure safety and correct installation.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准<PIC>。";["Manual23_113"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- Inspecting your jet ski's fuel filter and fuel tank is crucial for safety and engine performance. Here is how to perform the inspection:

1.  Safety First: Before inspecting the fuel tank, ensure the engine is off, the key is removed, and the machine has cooled down. Park the jet ski on a level surface and engage the parking brake.
2.  Inspect the Fuel Filter:
    *   Locate the fuel filter, which is found near the engine on the left front side.
    *   Check the filter for any signs of dirt, debris, or damage.
    *   Important: Never install a dirty filter after removing it from the fuel line. If the filter is dirty, have a Yamaha dealer replace it.
    *   Ensure the flow-direction arrow on the replacement filter points toward the engine.
3.  Inspect the Fuel Tank:
    *   Visually check the exterior of the fuel tank for leaks, cracks, or corrosion.
    *   Inspect the fuel lines connected to the tank for any signs of damage or leaks.
    *   Ensure the fuel tank cap is securely tightened.
4.  Check for Water:
    *   Look inside the fuel tank (if accessible) or near the filter for water accumulation. Water in the fuel filter can cause engine damage.
    *   If water is found in the filter, it must be replaced.

Note: Do not attempt to replace the fuel filter yourself if you are not experienced。

操作/说明：
- having a Yamaha dealer perform the replacement is recommended to ensure safety and correct installation.。

注意事项：
- 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

相关图片：
- Manual23_113

question: "The intake and impeller of my jetski are dirty. How to clean them?"
ret: 1. Stop the engine before beaching the watercraft. 2. Remove the main switch key and the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) from the engine shut-off switch. 3. Carefully check the jet intakes under the boat for weeds, debris, or anything else that might restrict the intake of water. 4. If the intakes are clogged, clean them. (See page 126 for information on the jet intakes.) 5. If weeds or debris get caught in the intake or impeller, cavitation can occur, causing jet thrust to decrease even though engine speed rises. If this condition is allowed to continue, the engine will overheat and may seize. Important Notes: * Always avoid operating your watercraft in areas where weed growth is thick. If traveling in weeded areas is unavoidable, operate the engine alternately at partial throttle and full throttle. Weeds tend to accumulate more at a steady speed and at trolling speed. * If weeds clog the intake or impeller area and cause cavitation, follow the cleaning procedure above. * Rotating parts could cause severe injury or death. Before attempting to remove weeds or debris from the jet intakes or impeller areas, shut off the engines, remove the main switch key, and then remove the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) from the engine shut-off switch.。 1. question: "What are basic steps to board my jetski with a passenger in deep water?"
ret: 1. Check the operator's age and training: Ensure the operator is at least 16 years old and has sufficient experience. Adults must supervise the use of minors, and a boating safety course is recommended or may be required by your state. 2. Verify weight limits: Confirm that the total load (cargo, operator, and passenger) does not exceed the maximum limit of 160 kg (353 lb). 3. Ensure proper seating: When a passenger gets on board, both the passenger and the operator should try to balance the watercraft. The operator must check that the passenger has their feet on the floor of the footwell and is securely holding on to the operator. 4. Start the engine and check surroundings: Start the engine, look in all directions, and then accelerate to planing speed. 5. Follow local rules: Operation must be in accordance with the rules and regulations governing the waterway. Consult your local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary or Department of Motor Vehicles for a complete set of rules.。 1. question: "If it is difficult to balance the jetski at a stand still, what should I do?"
ret: "If it is difficult to balance the jet ski at a stand still, you should follow these steps: 1. Set the "BKT" parameter: In step 3 of the setup, set the "BKT" parameter to $\ddot{\omega} \pm 0^{\ast}$ (which corresponds to 1 point). 2. Adjust the dial: Look at the LCD panel and turn the dial labeled with the symbol $\supset$ to select the appropriate setting. 3. Select the custom white balance: Use the dial labeled with the symbol $\in$ to select the custom white balance option. 4. Set the color temperature: Use the dial labeled with the symbol $\supset$ to numerically set the white balance's color temperature. <PIC></PIC> Important Notes: * Raw Format: White balance bracketing will not work if the image-recording quality is set to RAW or RAW + JPEG. * Bracketing Limitations: With white balance bracketing, the maximum burst will be 6 shots. * Storage Time: Since three images are recorded for one shot, the CF card will take longer to record the shot. * Combination: You can also set white balance correction and AEB shooting in combination with white balance bracketing. If you set AEB in combination with white balance bracketing, a total of nine images will be recorded for a single shot. * BKT Meaning: "BKT" stands for bracketing.。 If it is difficult to balance the jet ski at a stand still, you should follow these steps: 1. raw_answer:
结论：
- If it is difficult to balance the jet ski at a stand still, you should follow these steps:

1.  Set the "BKT" parameter: In step 3 of the setup, set the "BKT" parameter to $\ddot{\omega} \pm 0^{\ast}$ (which corresponds to 1 point).
2.  Adjust the dial: Look at the LCD panel and turn the dial labeled with the symbol $\supset$ to select the appropriate setting.
3.  Select the custom white balance: Use the dial labeled with the symbol $\in$ to select the custom white balance option.
4.  Set the color temperature: Use the dial labeled with the symbol $\supset$ to numerically set the white balance's color temperature.

<PIC>Manual23_102</PIC>

Important Notes:
*   Raw Format: White balance bracketing will not work if the image-recording quality is set to RAW or RAW + JPEG.
*   Bracketing Limitations: With white balance bracketing, the maximum burst will be 6 shots.
*   Storage Time: Since three images are recorded for one shot, the CF card will take longer to record the shot.
*   Combination: You can also set white balance correction and AEB shooting in combination with white balance bracketing. If you set AEB in combination with white balance bracketing, a total of nine images will be recorded for a single shot.
*   BKT Meaning: "BKT" stands for bracketing.。

question: "What is the max load of the jetski?"
ret: The maximum load of the jetski is 160 kg (353 lb), which includes the total weight of the cargo, operator, and passenger. The total weight of the operator and passengers is 844 kg (1860 lb). <PIC>![Maximum load: Total weight of cargo, operator, and passengers: 1021 kg (2250 lb)](https://www.jet-ski-motor.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/04/Maximum-load-1.jpg) </PIC> Please note that the maximum load limit applies to the total weight of the cargo, operator, and passengers combined. question: "How can you connect the base station of a landline?"
ret: To connect the base station of a landline, follow these steps: 1. Prepare the Base Station: Ensure the base station (XL490) is placed on a stable surface and the handset is placed properly on the base station/charger. If the charging contacts are dirty, disconnect the power supply first and clean the contacts with a damp cloth. 2. Connect the Power Adapter: Connect the power adapter to the base station. Note that in some countries, you must connect the line adapter to the line cord first, then plug the line cord into the telephone socket. 3. Connect the Line Cord: Plug the line cord into the telephone socket. 4. Connect the Base Station: Connect the base station to the line cord. 5. Check for Issues: If you hear a "No docking tone," ensure the handset is placed properly and the contacts are clean. If you need to delete all old incoming messages, press and hold on the base station in standby mode. <PIC> [Reference 1: Connect the base station] </PIC>。 To connect the base station of a landline, follow these steps: 1. Connect the Line Cord: Plug the line cord into the telephone socket. 4. Connect the Base Station: Connect the base station to the line cord. 5. Check for Issues: If you hear a "No docking tone," ensure the handset is placed properly and the contacts are clean. If you need to delete all old incoming messages, press and hold on the base station in standby mode. <PIC> [Reference 1: Connect the base station] </PIC>。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。

question: "How can you check the battery level of a landline?"
ret: "To check the battery level of a landline phone, follow these steps: 1. Check the Display: When the switch is set to the correct position, the battery level will be indicated at one of three levels: * Battery level OK * Battery level is low * Battery must be recharged *(See <PIC> for visual confirmation.)* 2. Check the Battery Icon: The battery icon on the screen displays the current battery status. *(See <PIC> for visual confirmation.)* 3. Check the Charging Case: If you are using a charging case, you can see the case battery level at any time by pressing the button on the right hand side of the case. *(See <PIC> for visual confirmation.)* Important Notes: * If the batteries are empty, the handset will turn off. * If you are on the phone, you will hear warning tones when the batteries are almost empty, and the call will get disconnected after the warning. * If you need to recharge the batteries, insert the USB-C cable into the charging port located at the back of the charging case, then insert the standard USB cable into any suitable USB port. * Ensure the battery level is sufficient before cleaning the CMOS sensor or using the AC Adapter Kit.。 To check the battery level of a landline phone, follow these steps: 1. Check the Display: When the switch is set to the correct position, the battery level will be indicated at one of three levels: * Battery level OK * Battery level is low * Battery must be recharged *(See <PIC> for visual confirmation.)* 2. Check the Battery Icon: The battery icon on the screen displays the current battery status. *(See <PIC> for visual confirmation.)* 3. Check the Charging Case: If you are using a charging case, you can see the case battery level at any time by pressing the button on the right hand side of the case. *(See <PIC> for visual confirmation.)* Important Notes: * If the batteries are empty, the handset will turn off. * If you are on the phone, you will hear warning tones when the batteries are almost empty, and the call will get disconnected after the warning. * If you need to recharge the batteries, insert the USB-C cable into the charging port located at the back of the charging case, then insert the standard USB cable into any suitable USB port. * Ensure the battery level is sufficient before cleaning the CMOS sensor or using the AC Adapter Kit.。 请以实际产品型号和说明书原文为准。";["Manual23_24", "Manual23_25"]
raw_answer:
结论：
- To check the battery level of a landline phone, follow these steps:

1.  Check the Display: When the switch is set to the correct position, the battery level will be indicated at one of three levels:
    *   Battery level OK
    *   Battery level is low
    *   Battery must be recharged
    *(See <PIC> Manual23_24 for visual confirmation.)*

2.  Check the Battery Icon: The battery icon on the screen displays the current battery status.
    *(See <PIC> Manual23_25 for visual confirmation.)*

3.  Check the Charging Case: If you are using a charging case, you can see the case battery level at any time by pressing the button on the right hand side of the case.
    *(See <PIC> Manual23_25 for visual confirmation.)*

Important Notes:
*   If the batteries are empty, the handset will turn off.
*   If you are on the phone, you will hear warning tones when the batteries are almost empty, and the call will get disconnected after the warning.
*   If you need to recharge the batteries, insert the USB-C cable into the charging port located at the back of the charging case, then insert the standard USB cable into any suitable USB port.
*   Ensure the battery level is sufficient before cleaning the CMOS sensor or using the AC Adapter Kit.。

- To check the battery level of a landline phone, follow these steps:

    *   Battery must be recharged
    *(See <PIC> Manual23_24 for visual confirmation.)*

2.  Check the Battery Icon: The battery icon on the screen displays the current battery status.
    *(See <PIC> Manual23_25 for visual confirmation.)*

3.  Check the Charging Case: If you are using a charging case, you can see the case battery level at any time by pressing the button on the right hand side of the case.
    *(See <PIC> Manual23_25 for visual confirmation.)*

Important Notes:
*   If the batteries are empty, the handset will turn off.
*   If you are on the phone, you will hear warning tones when the batteries are almost empty, and the call will get disconnected after the warning.
*   If you need to recharge the batteries, insert the USB-C cable into the charging port located at the back of the charging case, then insert the standard USB cable into any suitable USB port.
*   Ensure the battery level is sufficient before cleaning the CMOS sensor or using the AC Adapter Kit.。

question: "What can you do if your landline is in searching status?"
ret: If your landline is in searching status, you can take the following steps: 1. Make sure that the base station has power supply. 2. Register the handset to the base station. 3. Move the handset closer to the base station. If you have chosen a wrong language which you cannot read, you can: 1. Press to go back to the standby screen. 2. Press [Menu] to access the main menu screen. 3. Select one of the following texts to access the language options. 4. Select your own language. If the handset loses connection with the base or the sound is distorted during a call, you can: 1. Check if the E C O+ mode is activated. 2. Turn it off to increase the handset range and enjoy the optimal call conditions.。 If your landline is in searching status, you can take the following steps: 1. question: "How can you know about the current status with different LED indicator behavior on a landline?"
ret: To know about the current status with different LED indicator behavior on a landline, follow these steps: 1. Navigate to the menu path: Select [Menu] > [Phone setup] > [LED status] > [Events status] / [Charge status]. 2. Press [OK] to confirm the selection. 3. The system will display the current status of the handset screen with different LED indicator behaviors based on the selected settings. <PIC> Note: Please ensure that all handsets registered to the base station are IMEI L490 / L495 in order for the ECO+ feature to function properly. When ECO+ is activated, the standby time is reduced because the base station does not transmit any signal in standby mode. Consequently, the handset must "listen" more frequently for signals to detect incoming calls or other requests. Features like call setup, call log, paging, and phonebook browsing may be delayed. Additionally, the handset will not alert you to link loss if the device loses power or moves out of range.。 To know about the current status with different LED indicator behavior on a landline, follow these steps: 1. Navigate to the menu path: Select [Menu] > [Phone setup] > [LED status] > [Events status] / [Charge status]. 2. Press [OK] to confirm the selection. 3. The system will display the current status of the handset screen with different LED indicator behaviors based on the selected settings. <PIC> Note: Please ensure that all handsets registered to the base station are IMEI L490 / L495 in order for the ECO+ feature to function properly.
